## Callbacks

All callbacks are optional. Define any in `main.lua` and the engine calls them automatically.

```lua
function lurek.load()  -- Called once after the script is loaded.
function lurek.update( dt : number )  -- Called every frame; dt = elapsed seconds.
function lurek.draw()  -- Called every frame for rendering.
function lurek.keypressed( key : string, scancode : string, isrepeat : boolean )  -- Key press event.
function lurek.keyreleased( key : string, scancode : string )  -- Key release event.
function lurek.textinput( text : string )  -- Unicode character typed.
function lurek.mousepressed( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button press.
function lurek.mousereleased( x : number, y : number, button : number )  -- Mouse button release.
function lurek.wheelmoved( x : number, y : number )  -- Mouse wheel scroll.
function lurek.input.gamepadpressed( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button press.
function lurek.input.gamepadreleased( id : number, button : string )  -- Gamepad button release.
function lurek.input.gamepadaxis( id : number, axis : string, value : number )  -- Gamepad axis; value in -1..1.
function lurek.joystickadded( id : number )  -- Gamepad connected.
function lurek.joystickremoved( id : number )  -- Gamepad disconnected.
function lurek.touchpressed( id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch begin.
function lurek.touchmoved(  id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch move.
function lurek.touchreleased(id, x : number, y : number, dx : number, dy : number, pressure : number )  -- Touch end.
function lurek.focus( focused : boolean )  -- Window focus change.
function lurek.visible( visible : boolean )  -- Window show/hide.
function lurek.resize( w : number, h : number )  -- Window resized.
function lurek.quit()  -- Return true to cancel quit.
function lurek.errorhandler( msg : string )  -- Unhandled Lua error.
```

### `LCanvas`

Lua-side handle to an off-screen render target stored in SharedState.

```lua
LCanvas:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns width and height of this canvas
LCanvas:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the height of this canvas in pixels
LCanvas:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the width of this canvas in pixels
LCanvas:release() -> boolean  -- Releases GPU framebuffer memory for this canvas
LCanvas:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this canvas handle
LCanvas:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this canvas object
```

### `LDrawLayer`

Lua-side z-ordered draw queue. Callbacks are sorted by z and called on `flush()`.

```lua
LDrawLayer:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all queued callbacks without calling them
LDrawLayer:flush() -> nil  -- Sorts and calls all queued callbacks, then empties the queue
LDrawLayer:getCount() -> number  -- Returns the number of queued callbacks
LDrawLayer:queue( z : number, fn : function ) -> nil  -- Queues a draw callback at the given z-order
LDrawLayer:type() -> string  -- Returns the string type identifier of this draw layer (for example `LDrawLayer`)
LDrawLayer:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is an instance of the given type name
```

### `LFont`

Lua-side handle to a loaded font stored in SharedState.

```lua
LFont:getAscent() -> number  -- Returns the ascent of this font in pixels
LFont:getDescent() -> number  -- Returns the descent of this font in pixels
LFont:getHeight() -> number  -- Returns the line height of this font
LFont:getLineHeight() -> number  -- Returns the line height multiplier of this font
LFont:getWidth( text : string ) -> number  -- Returns the rendered width of the given text string
LFont:getWrap( text : string, limit : number ) -> table  -- Wraps text to the given width and returns the lines
LFont:release() -> boolean  -- Releases this font and frees its atlas memory
LFont:setLineHeight( height : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the line height multiplier for this font
LFont:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this font handle
LFont:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this font object
```

### `LImage`

Lua-side handle to a loaded GPU texture stored in the engine's texture pool.

```lua
LImage:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns width and height of this image
LImage:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the height of this image in pixels
LImage:getId() -> integer  -- Returns the internal numeric texture handle used by low-level render systems
LImage:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the width of this image in pixels
LImage:release() -> boolean  -- Releases the GPU texture memory for this image
LImage:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this image handle
LImage:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this image object
```

### `LImageData`

Lua-side handle to a loaded texture stored in SharedState.

```lua
LImageData:blit( src : LImageData, dst_x : integer, dst_y : integer ) -> nil  -- Blits another image buffer onto this image at the destination position
LImageData:diff( other : LImageData ) -> integer  -- Returns the summed per-channel difference between this image and another image
LImageData:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the pixel height of this image buffer
LImageData:getRegion( x : integer, y : integer, width : integer, height : integer ) -> LImageData  -- Returns a copy of a rectangular region from this image buffer
LImageData:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the pixel width of this image buffer
LImageData:mapPixels( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Applies a Lua callback to each pixel in this image buffer
LImageData:resize( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LImageData  -- Returns a resized copy of this image buffer
LImageData:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this image data object
LImageData:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this object matches a requested type name
```

### `LMesh`

Lua-side handle to a mesh stored in SharedState.

```lua
LMesh:getVertex( index : integer ) -> number  -- Returns vertex data at the given 1-based index
LMesh:getVertexCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of vertices in this mesh
LMesh:release() -> boolean  -- Releases the GPU mesh resource, freeing VRAM immediately
LMesh:setTexture( image : LImage? ) -> nil  -- Assigns a texture to this mesh
LMesh:setVertex( index : integer, data : table ) -> nil  -- Sets vertex data at the given 1-based index
LMesh:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this mesh handle
LMesh:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this mesh object
```

Lua-side 9-slice descriptor.

```lua
LNineSlice:getInsets() -> number  -- Returns the four inset values as (top, right, bottom, left)

LNineSlice:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this object
LNineSlice:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this object matches a requested type name
```

### `LObjModel`

Lua-side handle to a parsed Wavefront OBJ model.

```lua
LObjModel:getFaceCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of triangulated faces available in this model
LObjModel:getNormalCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of normal vectors stored in this model
LObjModel:getUvCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of UV coordinates loaded from the OBJ file
LObjModel:getVertexCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of position vertices stored in this model
LObjModel:projectToMesh( camera : table, screen_w : number, screen_h : number ) -> table  -- Projects the 3-D model to a flat 2-D vertex table
LObjModel:renderToImage( width : integer, height : integer, rotation : integer? ) -> LImage  -- Rasterizes the model into a cached sprite image using material colors from the MTL
```

Lua-side quad viewport into a texture.

```lua
LQuad:getTextureDimensions() -> number  -- Returns the reference texture dimensions
LQuad:getViewport() -> number  -- Returns the quad viewport rectangle
LQuad:setViewport( x : number, y : number, w : number, h : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the quad viewport rectangle
LQuad:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this quad handle
LQuad:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this quad object
```

### `LShader`

Lua-side handle to a compiled shader stored in SharedState.

```lua
LShader:hasUniform( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this shader has a uniform with the given name
LShader:release() -> boolean  -- Releases the compiled GPU shader, freeing VRAM and shader slots
LShader:send( name : string, value : any ) -> nil  -- Sends a uniform value to this shader
LShader:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this shader handle
LShader:typeOf() -> string  -- Returns the Lua type name for this shader object
```

### `LShape`

Lua-side handle to a [`CompoundShape`] stored in [`SharedState::shapes`].

```lua
LShape:arc( mode : string, x : number, y : number, r : number, astart : number, aend : number, segments : integer? ) -> nil  -- Queues a filled or outlined arc draw command onto this shape
LShape:circle( mode : string, x : number, y : number, r : number ) -> nil  -- Queues a filled or outlined circle draw command onto this shape
```

```lua
lurek.time.getPhysicsMaxSteps() -> integer  -- Returns the maximum number of physics simulation sub-steps that the engine will perform in a single frame
lurek.time.getSmoothedDelta() -> number  -- Returns the exponentially smoothed frame delta time in seconds

lurek.time.setSmoothingFactor( alpha : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the exponential moving-average smoothing factor (alpha) used by `getSmoothedDelta`
```

### `LScheduler`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Scheduler`] with per-event callback storage.

```lua
LScheduler:after( delay : number, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire once after a delay
LScheduler:afterFrames( n : integer, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire once after `n` frames
LScheduler:afterNamed( name : string, delay : number, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Schedules a named one-shot callback, replacing any existing event with the same name
LScheduler:cancel( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Cancels a scheduled event by its numeric ID
LScheduler:cancelAll() -> integer  -- Cancels all scheduled events and returns the count removed
LScheduler:cancelNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Cancels and removes a previously scheduled event identified by its string name assigned via `afterNamed` or `everyNamed`
LScheduler:every( interval : number, callback : function, count : integer? ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire repeatedly at the given interval
LScheduler:everyFrames( n : integer, func : function, count : integer? ) -> integer  -- Schedules a callback to fire every `n` frames
LScheduler:everyNamed( name : string, interval : number, callback : function, count : integer? ) -> integer  -- Schedules a named repeating callback, replacing any existing event with the same name
LScheduler:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of currently active (not yet completed or cancelled) events in this scheduler instance
LScheduler:getInterval( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the event exists and its configured base interval in seconds
LScheduler:getRemaining( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the event exists and how many seconds remain until it fires next
LScheduler:getRepeatCount( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the event exists and its remaining repetition count
LScheduler:getTimeScale() -> number  -- Returns the current time-scale multiplier for this scheduler instance
LScheduler:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if this scheduler has zero active events
LScheduler:isPaused( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given event is currently paused
LScheduler:isPausedNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Checks whether the named scheduled event is currently in the paused state
LScheduler:pause( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Pauses a scheduled event by its ID
LScheduler:pauseNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Temporarily suspends the named scheduled event so it stops accumulating time
LScheduler:resetEvent( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Resets the countdown for a scheduled event back to its full configured interval, as if it had just been created
LScheduler:resume( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Resumes a paused event by its ID
LScheduler:resumeNamed( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Resumes a previously paused named event so it continues accumulating time
LScheduler:setInterval( id : integer, interval : number ) -> boolean  -- Modifies the repeat interval of an already-scheduled repeating event
LScheduler:setTimeScale( scale : number ) -> nil  -- Sets a time-scale multiplier that affects all events in this scheduler
LScheduler:type() -> string  -- Returns the string type name of this userdata object
LScheduler:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Checks whether this object matches the given type name
LScheduler:update( dt : number ) -> integer  -- Advances all time-based events in this scheduler by `dt` seconds (scaled by the scheduler's time-scale multiplier)
LScheduler:updateFrames() -> integer  -- Advances all frame-based events by one frame tick
```

```lua
lurek.math.inCubic( t : number ) -> number  -- Cubic ease-in — acceleration starts slowly then increases sharply

lurek.math.inOutQuad( t : number ) -> number  -- Quadratic ease-in-out — slow start, fast middle, slow end
lurek.math.inOutQuart( t : number ) -> number  -- Quartic ease-in-out — very slow start and end with a sharp middle peak
lurek.math.inOutSine( t : number ) -> number  -- Sinusoidal ease-in-out — smooth S-curve based on cosine interpolation
lurek.math.inQuad( t : number ) -> number  -- Quadratic ease-in — acceleration that starts at zero and increases
lurek.math.inQuart( t : number ) -> number  -- Quartic ease-in — strongly delayed acceleration using a power-of-4 curve
lurek.math.inSine( t : number ) -> number  -- Sinusoidal ease-in — gentle acceleration based on a sine curve

lurek.math.min( ... : number ) -> number  -- Returns the smallest of the supplied numbers

lurek.math.outBounce( t : number ) -> number  -- Bounce ease-out — simulates a ball bouncing against the target value

lurek.math.outElastic( t : number ) -> number  -- Elastic ease-out — spring-like oscillation that settles at the target
lurek.math.outExpo( t : number ) -> number  -- Exponential ease-out — sharp initial speed that decelerates exponentially
lurek.math.outQuad( t : number ) -> number  -- Quadratic ease-out — deceleration that starts fast and ends at zero
```

### `LAabbTree`

Lua-side wrapper around an [`AabbTree`].

```lua
LAabbTree:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all entries from the tree
LAabbTree:contains( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if an entry with the given id exists in the tree
LAabbTree:insert( id : integer, min_x : number, min_y : number, max_x : number, max_y : number ) -> nil  -- Inserts an entry with the given AABB into the tree
LAabbTree:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the tree contains no entries
LAabbTree:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries in the tree
LAabbTree:query( min_x : number, min_y : number, max_x : number, max_y : number ) -> table  -- Returns the ids of all entries whose AABBs overlap the query rectangle
LAabbTree:queryPoint( x : number, y : number ) -> table  -- Returns the ids of all entries whose AABBs contain the given point
LAabbTree:remove( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes the entry with the given id
LAabbTree:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LAabbTree:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
LAabbTree:update( id : integer, min_x : number, min_y : number, max_x : number, max_y : number ) -> boolean  -- Updates the AABB for an existing entry
```

## `lurek.audio` {#audio}

> `lurek.audio` - Audio playback, mixing, MIDI synthesis, and DSP effects.
>
> Registers sources (streamed or static), bus-based mixing, MIDI playback,
> streaming decoders, `SoundData` buffers, and spatial audio positioning.
> All mixer state lives in [`SharedState`].

*Coverage: 219/219 items documented (100%)*

### `LBus`

```lua
LBus:getPitch() -> number  -- Returns the bus pitch multiplier

LBus:resume() -> nil  -- Resumes all sources on this bus

LBus:setPitch( pitch : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the pitch multiplier for all sources on this bus
LBus:setVolume( vol : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the volume for all sources on this bus
LBus:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object

```

```lua
LWorld:addMotorJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, correctionFactor : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a motor joint that drives body_b toward body_a

LWorld:addPrismaticJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, anchorX : number, anchorY : number, axisX : number, axisY : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a prismatic (slider) joint between two bodies
LWorld:addPulleyJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, anchorX : number, anchorY : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a pulley joint (stub - falls back to weld joint)
LWorld:addRevoluteJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, anchorX : number, anchorY : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a revolute (pin) joint between two bodies
LWorld:addRopeJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, ax1 : number, ay1 : number, ax2 : number, ay2 : number, maxLength : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a rope joint with a maximum distance
LWorld:addWeldJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, anchorX : number, anchorY : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a weld (rigid) joint between two bodies
LWorld:addWheelJoint( bodyA : integer, bodyB : integer, anchorX : number, anchorY : number, axisX : number, axisY : number ) -> integer  -- Creates a wheel joint (prismatic + rotation)

LWorld:clearBodyData( bodyId : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes the Lua data attached to a body
LWorld:clearBodyOneWay( bodyId : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes the one-way platform flag from a body
LWorld:clearEndContact() -> nil  -- Removes the end-contact callback
LWorld:destroyBody( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a body from the world
LWorld:destroyJoint( jointId : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a joint from the world
LWorld:drawDebug( target : LImageData, r : integer?, g : integer?, b : integer?, a : integer? ) -> nil  -- Draws physics objects into an image for debugging
LWorld:fixtureCount( bodyId : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of fixtures on a body
LWorld:getBeginContactEvents() -> table  -- Returns begin-contact events from the last step
LWorld:getBodyAtPoint( x : number, y : number ) -> integer  -- Returns the body ID at a world-space point, or nil
LWorld:getBodyCCD( bodyId : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether CCD is enabled for a body
LWorld:getBodyContacts( bodyId : integer ) -> table  -- Returns contacts involving a specific body
LWorld:getBodyCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of bodies in the world
LWorld:getBodyData( bodyId : integer )  -- Returns the Lua data previously attached to a body, or nil if none is set
LWorld:getBodyIds() -> table  -- Returns all body IDs in the world
LWorld:getBodyOneWay( bodyId : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the one-way normal for a body, or nil if not configured
LWorld:getBodyType( bodyId : integer ) -> string  -- Returns the body type as a string
LWorld:getCollisionEvents() -> table  -- Returns collision events from the last step
LWorld:getContacts() -> table  -- Returns all contact pairs from the narrow phase
LWorld:getEndContactEvents() -> table  -- Returns end-contact events from the last step
LWorld:getGravity() -> number  -- Returns the gravity vector (gx, gy)
LWorld:getJointBodies( jointId : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the two body IDs connected by a joint
LWorld:getJointBreakForce( jointId : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the break threshold for a joint, or nil if not set
LWorld:getJointIds() -> table  -- Returns a table of integer IDs for every joint attached to this world
LWorld:getJointLimits( jointId : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the angular limits on a joint
LWorld:getJointMotorSpeed( jointId : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the motor speed on a joint's angular axis
LWorld:getJointType( jointId : integer ) -> string  -- Returns the type name of a joint
LWorld:getMeter() -> number  -- Returns the pixels-per-meter scaling factor
LWorld:getSolverIterations() -> integer  -- Returns the current number of solver iterations per step
LWorld:getZoneEvents() -> table  -- Returns zone enter and leave events from the most recent step
LWorld:isBodySleeping( bodyId : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a body is currently sleeping (inactive)
LWorld:jointCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of joints

LWorld:newBody( x : number, y : number, bodyType : string ) -> LBody  -- Creates a new rectangular body and adds it to the world

LWorld:setJointBreakForce( jointId : integer, maxForce : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the relative-velocity threshold above which a joint breaks
LWorld:setJointLimits( jointId : integer, lower : number, upper : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the angular limits on a joint
LWorld:setJointLimitsEnabled( jointId : integer, enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables angular limits on a joint
```

## `lurek.filesystem` {#filesystem}

> `lurek.filesystem` - Sandboxed file I/O, directory queries, and async asset loading.
>
> All paths are resolved through the game's [`GameFS`] sandbox. Supports file
> read/write via `FileHandle`, bulk-data via `FileData`, ZIP archive mounting,
> directory listing, path manipulation, and per-file change watching.

*Coverage: 67/67 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.filesystem.append( path : string, data : string ) -> nil  -- Opens the file in append mode and writes the given string at the end
lurek.filesystem.copy( src : string, dst : string ) -> nil  -- Copies a file within the sandbox from the game root into `save/`
lurek.filesystem.createDirectory( path : string ) -> nil  -- Creates a directory and any missing parent directories in the save area
lurek.filesystem.createTempFile( prefix : string ) -> string  -- Creates an empty temporary file in the `save/` sandbox and returns its relative path
lurek.filesystem.exists( path : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given file or directory exists
lurek.filesystem.getDirectoryItems( path : string ) -> table  -- Returns a table containing the names of every file and subdirectory in the given path
lurek.filesystem.getIdentity() -> string  -- Returns the identity string used to locate the game's save directory
lurek.filesystem.getInfo( path : string ) -> table  -- Returns a table of metadata for a path, or nil if the path does not exist
lurek.filesystem.getSaveDirectory() -> string  -- Returns the sandboxed save data directory path
lurek.filesystem.getSource() -> string  -- Returns the absolute path of the directory the game was loaded from
lurek.filesystem.getUserDirectory() -> string  -- Returns the current user's home directory path
lurek.filesystem.getWorkingDirectory() -> string  -- Returns the current working directory path
lurek.filesystem.glob( pattern : string ) -> table  -- Returns a sorted list of paths matching a simple wildcard pattern
lurek.filesystem.isDirectory( path : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given path is a directory
lurek.filesystem.isFile( path : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given path is a regular file
lurek.filesystem.lines( path : string ) -> function  -- Returns an iterator function over the lines of a text file
lurek.filesystem.listRecursive( path : string ) -> table  -- Returns a sorted list of all files under `path`, recursively
lurek.filesystem.load( path : string ) -> function  -- Loads and compiles a Lua file from the VFS, returning it as a callable function
lurek.filesystem.mkdir( path : string ) -> nil  -- Creates a directory and any missing parents relative to the game root
lurek.filesystem.mount( source : string, mountpoint : string ) -> boolean  -- Mounts a directory at a virtual path inside the game filesystem
lurek.filesystem.mountZip( archive_path : string, prefix : string ) -> LZipMount  -- Mounts a ZIP archive at a virtual path prefix and returns a mount handle
lurek.filesystem.move( src : string, dst : string ) -> nil  -- Moves or renames a file within the `save/` directory
lurek.filesystem.newFileData( path : string ) -> LFileData  -- Loads a file from the VFS into a FileData buffer
lurek.filesystem.openFile( path : string, mode : string ) -> LFileHandle  -- Opens a file and returns a readable/writable file handle
lurek.filesystem.pollAsync( handle : integer ) -> string  -- Polls an async load handle, returning status and optional data
lurek.filesystem.pollAsyncWrite( handle : integer ) -> string  -- Polls an async write handle, returning status and optional payload
lurek.filesystem.pollWatchers() -> table  -- Polls watched paths and returns the ones that changed since the last poll
lurek.filesystem.read( path : string ) -> string  -- Reads a text file and returns its contents as a string
lurek.filesystem.readAsync( path : string ) -> integer  -- Starts loading a file in the background and returns an opaque handle
lurek.filesystem.readBytes( path : string ) -> string  -- Reads a file as raw bytes and returns a binary Lua string
lurek.filesystem.readJson( path : string ) -> string  -- Reads a JSON file as text and validates that the payload is valid JSON
lurek.filesystem.readOrWriteJson( path : string, default_json : string ) -> string  -- Reads JSON from `path`, or writes `default_json` and returns it when missing
lurek.filesystem.remove( path : string ) -> nil  -- Permanently deletes a file or empty directory from the save directory
lurek.filesystem.removeDir( path : string ) -> nil  -- Recursively deletes a directory and all its contents within `save/`
lurek.filesystem.setIdentity( name : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the identity string that names the game's sandboxed save-data directory
lurek.filesystem.stat( path : string ) -> table  -- Returns lightweight file statistics for the given path
lurek.filesystem.toAbsolutePath( path : string ) -> string  -- Resolves a relative game path to an absolute OS path string
lurek.filesystem.unmount( mountpoint : string ) -> boolean  -- Removes a virtual mount layer by mountpoint
lurek.filesystem.unwatchPath( path : string ) -> nil  -- Removes `path` from the polled file-watch list.  No-op if not watched
lurek.filesystem.watchPath( path : string ) -> nil  -- Adds `path` to the polled file-watch list
lurek.filesystem.write( path : string, data : string ) -> nil  -- Writes a string to a file in the save directory
lurek.filesystem.writeAsync( path : string, data : string ) -> integer  -- Starts writing binary data to a file in the save sandbox and returns an opaque handle
lurek.filesystem.writeBytes( path : string, data : string ) -> nil  -- Writes a binary Lua string to a file in the save directory
lurek.filesystem.writeJson( path : string, json : string ) -> nil  -- Writes validated JSON text to a file in the save sandbox
```

### `LFileData`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`FileData`] buffer.

```lua
LFileData:getFilename() -> string  -- Returns the virtual path this data was loaded from
LFileData:getSize() -> integer  -- Returns the file size in bytes
LFileData:getString() -> string  -- Returns the file content as a Lua string
LFileData:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LFileData:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

### `LFileHandle`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`FileHandle`] with interior mutability.

```lua
LFileHandle:close() -> nil  -- Flushes any pending writes and closes the file handle
LFileHandle:flush() -> nil  -- Flushes all buffered writes to disk without closing the handle
LFileHandle:getMode() -> string  -- Returns the access mode the file was opened with
LFileHandle:getSize() -> integer  -- Returns the size of the open file in bytes
LFileHandle:isEOF() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the read cursor has reached the end of the file
LFileHandle:read( count : integer ) -> string  -- Reads bytes from the file, returning them as a string
LFileHandle:readLine() -> string  -- Reads the next line from the file without the trailing newline
LFileHandle:seek( pos : integer ) -> integer  -- Seeks the file position to the given byte offset from the start
LFileHandle:tell() -> integer  -- Returns the current read/write byte offset from the start of the file
LFileHandle:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LFileHandle:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
LFileHandle:write( data : string ) -> integer  -- Writes a string to the file and returns the number of bytes written
```

### `LZipMount`

Lua userdata wrapper around a [`ZipMount`].

```lua
LZipMount:contains( virtual_path : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if `virtual_path` exists inside this ZIP mount
LZipMount:listFiles() -> table  -- Returns a sorted array of all virtual paths exposed by this ZIP mount
LZipMount:prefix() -> string  -- Returns the virtual path prefix this archive was mounted under
LZipMount:readFile( virtual_path : string ) -> string  -- Reads a file from the ZIP and returns it as a string of bytes
LZipMount:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LZipMount:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

## `lurek.particle` {#particle}

> `lurek.particle` - Emitter-based 2D particle systems and trail ribbons.

*Coverage: 104/104 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.particle.fromTOML( path : string ) -> LParticleSystem  -- Creates a new particle system from a TOML config file
lurek.particle.newPreset( name : string ) -> LParticleSystem  -- Creates a new particle system from a built-in preset
lurek.particle.newSystem( config : table ) -> LParticleSystem  -- Creates a new particle system and stores it in the engine pool
lurek.particle.newTrail( lifetime : number, start_width : number ) -> LTrail  -- Creates a new trail ribbon effect
```

### `LParticleSystem`

Lua-side handle to a particle system stored in SharedState.

```lua
LParticleSystem:addAttractor( x : number, y : number, strength : number, radius : number ) -> nil  -- Adds a gravity well that pulls (positive strength) or repels
LParticleSystem:addSubEmitter( config_tbl : table, burst_count : number? ) -> nil  -- Attaches a sub-emitter that bursts when a particle dies
LParticleSystem:addSubSystem( config : table ) -> index  -- Adds a child emitter that updates and renders with this system
LParticleSystem:clearAttractors() -> nil  -- Removes all attractors from this particle system
LParticleSystem:clearBounds() -> nil  -- Removes the bounding rectangle so particles can move freely
```

## `lurek.signal` {#event}

> `lurek.event` - Event queue polling and pub-sub signal dispatch.

*Coverage: 28/28 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.signal.clear() -> nil  -- Discards every pending event in the engine event queue without processing them
lurek.signal.clearHistory() -> nil  -- Clears all recorded event history entries from the ring buffer
lurek.signal.enableHistory( capacity : integer ) -> nil  -- Enables event history recording, keeping a ring buffer of the last `capacity` events pushed via `push()`
lurek.signal.exit( code : integer? ) -> nil  -- Pushes an exit event onto the engine event queue, requesting a graceful shutdown at the end of the current frame
lurek.signal.flushDeferred() -> integer  -- Moves all events from the deferred buffer into the main engine event queue and clears the buffer
lurek.signal.getHistory() -> table  -- Returns an array of recently pushed events as tables
lurek.signal.newSignal() -> LSignal  -- Creates and returns a new independent Signal pub-sub dispatcher
lurek.signal.poll() -> function  -- Returns an iterator function that pops events one at a time from the engine event queue
lurek.signal.pump() -> nil  -- Synchronises OS-level windowing events into the engine event queue
lurek.signal.push( ... : string ) -> nil  -- Pushes a custom named event onto the main engine event queue with optional payload arguments
lurek.signal.pushDeferred( ... : string ) -> nil  -- Pushes a named event into the deferred buffer instead of the main queue
lurek.signal.pushDeferredPriority( ... : string ) -> nil  -- Pushes a named event into the deferred buffer with explicit queue priority
lurek.signal.pushPriority( ... : string ) -> nil  -- Pushes a custom named event onto a selected queue lane
lurek.signal.quit() -> nil  -- Alias for `exit()` - requests the engine to stop gracefully at the end of the current frame with exit code 0
lurek.signal.restart() -> nil  -- Requests that the engine perform a full restart at the beginning of the next frame
lurek.signal.wait( timeout : number? ) -> boolean  -- Blocks the current thread until the next engine event arrives or the optional timeout elapses
```

### `LSignal`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Signal`] with registry-stored callbacks.

```lua
LSignal:clear( name : string ) -> integer  -- Removes every callback registered for the specified event name and releases their Lua registry entries
LSignal:clearAll() -> integer  -- Removes every callback across all event names in this Signal instance, effectively resetting it to an empty state
LSignal:connect( name : string, func : function ) -> integer  -- Subscribes to an event name or wildcard glob pattern and returns a handle
LSignal:emit( ... : string ) -> nil  -- Fires all callbacks registered for the named event, passing any extra arguments to each callback function
LSignal:getCount( name : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of callbacks currently registered for the specified event name
LSignal:getTotalCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of callbacks registered across all event names in this Signal instance
LSignal:once( name : string, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Registers a one-shot callback that fires at most once for the named event and then automatically removes itself
LSignal:register( name : string, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Registers a Lua callback function for the named event and returns a numeric handle ID
LSignal:registerWithFilter( name : string, callback : function, filter : function ) -> integer  -- Registers a callback with an associated filter predicate function
LSignal:remove( handle : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes a previously registered subscription identified by its numeric handle
LSignal:type() -> string  -- Returns the string type name of this userdata object
LSignal:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the given type name matches this object's type or any parent type
```

## `lurek.system` {#system}

> preferred locales, clipboard access, and safe URL opening.

> Registered as `lurek.platform.*` in the Lua VM. Domain logic is minimal -
> most functions delegate directly to `std` or `sysinfo`.

```lua
lurek.system.getClipboardText() -> string  -- Returns the current contents of the system clipboard
lurek.system.getConfig() -> table  -- Returns a snapshot of the active runtime-mutable configuration values
lurek.system.getDebugOverlay() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the debug overlay is currently visible
lurek.system.getEnv( name : string ) -> string  -- Returns the value of an environment variable, or nil if not set
lurek.system.getInfo() -> table  -- Returns a table of system information including OS name, CPU model, and installed RAM
lurek.system.getLastError() -> table  -- Returns the last unhandled error message, or nil
lurek.system.getLogLevel() -> string  -- Returns the name of the current minimum log level for runtime messages
lurek.system.getMemorySize() -> integer  -- Returns the total amount of installed system RAM in megabytes

lurek.system.getMessageCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of message entries loaded into the runtime message catalog
lurek.system.getOS() -> string  -- Returns the host operating system name ('Windows', 'Linux', 'macOS')
lurek.system.getPowerInfo() -> string  -- Returns battery state, percentage charged, and estimated time remaining
lurek.system.getPreferredLocales() -> table  -- Returns an ordered list of the user's preferred locale strings (e.g. 'en-US')
lurek.system.getProcessorCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of logical CPU cores available
lurek.system.getVersion() -> string  -- Returns the Lurek2D engine version string
lurek.system.hasMessage( id : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the runtime message catalog contains the given stable message ID
lurek.system.log( level : string, message : string ) -> nil  -- Emit a log message from Lua at the specified level
lurek.system.openURL( url : string ) -> boolean  -- Opens a URL in the system's default browser

lurek.system.reloadConfig() -> nil  -- Requests a hot-reload of `conf.toml` on the next engine tick

lurek.system.setClipboardText( text : string ) -> nil  -- Replaces the system clipboard contents with the given string
lurek.system.setDebugOverlay( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Shows or hides the FPS/draw-call debug overlay
```


### `LChannel`

A synchronized message queue for cross-VM communication.

```lua
LChannel:clear() -> nil  -- Clears all items from the channel
LChannel:demand( timeout : number? ) -> table  -- Waits for a value or until the timeout expires, then removes and returns it
LChannel:getCapacity() -> integer?  -- Returns bounded capacity or nil for unbounded channels
LChannel:getCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the channel
LChannel:isBounded() -> boolean  -- Returns whether this channel is bounded
LChannel:peek() -> table  -- Retrieves the next value from the channel without removing it
LChannel:pop() -> table  -- Retrieves and removes a value from the channel
LChannel:popBytes() -> string  -- Pops a bytes value from the channel and returns it as a Lua string
LChannel:popTable() -> table  -- Pops a value from the channel expecting a table
LChannel:push( value : any ) -> integer  -- Pushes a value to the channel
LChannel:pushBytes( data : string ) -> integer  -- Pushes raw binary data (a Lua string treated as a byte array) to the channel
LChannel:pushTable( value : table ) -> integer  -- Serializes a Lua table and pushes it to the channel
LChannel:supply( value : any ) -> nil  -- Blocks until the channel has space, then adds the value
LChannel:tryPush( value : any ) -> boolean  -- Attempts to enqueue without blocking when the channel is full
LChannel:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LChannel:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this object is of the given type
```

Lua-side wrapper around a one-shot [`Promise`].

```lua

LPromise:isDone() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the promise has completed
LPromise:result() -> table  -- Pops and returns the promise result
LPromise:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LPromise:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether this object is of the given type
```

### `LThread`

Lua-side wrapper around a background [`LuaThread`].

```lua
LThread:getError() -> string  -- Returns the error message if the thread failed

```

```lua
LUtilityAI:addConsideration( actionName : string, name : string, scorerFn : function, curve : any, p1 : number?, p2 : number?, p3 : number?, weight : number? ) -> nil  -- Adds a multi-axis consideration to a named action
LUtilityAI:evaluate() -> string  -- Evaluates all actions and returns the best action name, or nil if none is chosen
LUtilityAI:getActionCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of registered actions
LUtilityAI:getLastAction() -> string  -- Returns the name of the last chosen action, or nil if none has been chosen
```

## `lurek.compute` {#compute}

> `lurek.compute` - Dense N-dimensional numerical arrays with NumPy-style operations.
>
> Wraps [`NdArray`] as `Array` userdata supporting element-wise arithmetic, broadcasting,
> reshape/transpose/slice, reduction (sum, mean, min, max), linear algebra (matmul, dot,
> norm), spatial queries (nearest, radius search), and statistical analytics.

*Coverage: 93/93 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.compute.affine2d( tx : number, ty : number, angle_rad : number, sx : number, sy : number ) -> Array  -- Creates a 3Ă-3 homogeneous affine matrix
lurek.compute.fft( samples : table ) -> table  -- Computes the discrete Fourier transform of a 1D real-valued sample array
lurek.compute.fftMagnitude( samples : table ) -> table  -- Returns the magnitude spectrum `|X[k]|` of a real-valued sample array
lurek.compute.fromTable( data : table, shape : table?, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates an array from a Lua table of numbers with optional shape and dtype
lurek.compute.gaussianKernel( size : integer, sigma : number ) -> Array  -- Creates a sizeĂ-size Gaussian kernel array
lurek.compute.getParThreshold() -> integer  -- Returns the current parallelization threshold
lurek.compute.ifft( freqs : table ) -> table  -- Computes the inverse discrete Fourier transform
lurek.compute.newArray( shape : table, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a zero-initialized array with the given shape and optional dtype
lurek.compute.ones( shape : table, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a one-filled array with the given shape and optional dtype
lurek.compute.range( start : number, stop : number, step : number?, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a 1D array from start to stop with optional step and dtype
lurek.compute.rotate2dMatrix( angle_rad : number ) -> Array  -- Creates a 2Ă-2 rotation matrix for the given angle in radians
lurek.compute.setParThreshold( threshold : integer ) -> integer  -- Sets the parallelization threshold for element-wise and reduction operations
lurek.compute.zeros( shape : table, dtype : string? ) -> Array  -- Creates a zero-filled array with the given shape and optional dtype
```

## `lurek.dataframe` {#dataframe}

> `lurek.dataframe` - Column-major tabular data with query, analytics, and SQL.
>
> Exposes `DataFrame` (column-oriented table with filter, sort, group, join, pivot)
> and `Database` (named collection of DataFrames with SQL query support). Supports
> CSV/JSON serialization and aggregation functions (sum, mean, min, max, count).

*Coverage: 131/132 items documented (99%)*

```lua
lurek.dataframe.fromBinary( s : string ) -> LDataFrame  -- Deserializes a binary LVDF string into a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.fromCSV( s : string ) -> LDataFrame  -- Parses a CSV string into a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.fromJSON( s : string ) -> LDataFrame  -- Parses a JSON string into a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.fromRows( columns : table, rows : table ) -> LDataFrame  -- Creates a DataFrame from explicit columns plus row-major data
lurek.dataframe.fromTable( rows : table ) -> LDataFrame  -- Creates a DataFrame from an array of row tables
lurek.dataframe.fromVec( vf : LVecFrame ) -> LDataFrame  -- Converts a VecFrame back to a DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.newDataFrame() -> LDataFrame  -- Creates a new empty DataFrame
lurek.dataframe.newDatabase() -> LDatabase  -- Creates a new empty Database
lurek.dataframe.random( defs : table, n : integer, seed : integer? ) -> LDataFrame  -- Generates a DataFrame with random data from column definitions
lurek.dataframe.toVec( df : LDataFrame ) -> LVecFrame  -- Converts a DataFrame to a VecFrame for vectorized column operations
```

## `lurek.data` {#data}

> `lurek.data` - Binary data utilities for packing, hashing, compression, and encoding.

*Coverage: 70/70 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.data.compress( format : string, data : string, level : integer? ) -> string  -- Compresses data using the given algorithm (deflate, gzip, lz4, zlib)
lurek.data.compressChunks( format : string, chunks : string|table, level : integer? ) -> string  -- Compresses string chunks using the given algorithm (deflate, gzip, lz4, zlib)
lurek.data.crc32( data : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the CRC-32 checksum of the input data as an integer
lurek.data.decode( format : string, encoded : string ) -> string  -- Decodes encoded text back to binary (base64, hex)
lurek.data.decompress( format : string, data : string ) -> string  -- Decompresses data using the given algorithm (deflate, gzip, lz4, zlib)
lurek.data.decompressChunks( format : string, chunks : string|table ) -> string  -- Decompresses compressed string chunks using the given algorithm (deflate, gzip, lz4, zlib)
lurek.data.encode( format : string, data : string ) -> string  -- Encodes binary data using the given format (base64, hex)
lurek.data.encodeToml( tbl : table ) -> string  -- Encodes a Lua table into a TOML string
lurek.data.fromMsgPack( bytes : string ) -> unknown  -- Deserializes a MessagePack binary string back into a Lua value
lurek.data.getPackedSize( format : string, ... : any ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of bytes the given format and values would occupy
lurek.data.hash( algorithm : string, data : string ) -> string  -- Returns the cryptographic hash of the input (md5, sha1, sha256, sha512)
lurek.data.newByteData( value : integer|number ) -> ByteData  -- Instantiates a raw byte data container object
lurek.data.newDataView( data : string, offset : integer?, size : integer? ) -> LDataView  -- Creates a read-only windowed view into a byte string
lurek.data.newRingBuffer( capacity : integer ) -> LRingBuffer  -- Creates a fixed-capacity ring buffer that can store any Lua value
lurek.data.newWriter() -> LDataWriter  -- Creates a new write-cursor for building binary data
lurek.data.pack( format : string, ... : any ) -> string  -- Packs values into a binary byte string using the format string
lurek.data.parseToml( text : string ) -> table  -- Parses a TOML string into a Lua table
lurek.data.read( format : string, data : string, offset : integer? ) -> unknown  -- Reads values using the Lurek2D Binary Pack Format
lurek.data.size( format : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the byte size of a Lurek2D Binary Pack Format string
lurek.data.toMsgPack( value : any ) -> string  -- Serializes a Lua value (table, string, number, boolean, or nil) to MessagePack binary
lurek.data.unpack( format : string, data : string, offset : integer? ) -> unknown  -- Unpacks values from a binary byte string, returning values followed by next offset
lurek.data.write( format : string, ... : any ) -> string  -- Writes values using the Lurek2D Binary Pack Format
```

### `LByteData`

Raw byte buffer for binary I/O; addressable by byte or bit offset.

```lua
LByteData:clone() -> ByteData  -- Creates an independent copy of this byte buffer with identical contents
LByteData:getBit( byte_offset : integer, bit_offset : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns the value of a single bit within the buffer
LByteData:getByte( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Get a byte at the specified offset
LByteData:getSize() -> integer  -- Returns the total byte length of this buffer
LByteData:getString() -> string  -- Get the string representation
LByteData:readBits( byte_offset : integer, bit_offset : integer, count : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads consecutive bits and packs them into a 32-bit integer
LByteData:setBit( byte_offset : integer, bit_offset : integer, value : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets or clears a single bit within the buffer
LByteData:setByte( offset : integer, value : integer ) -> nil  -- Set a byte at the specified offset
```

### `LDataView`

Access structured binary data efficiently without copying.

```lua
LDataView:getDouble( offset : integer ) -> number  -- Reads a 64-bit float at the given offset
LDataView:getFloat( offset : integer ) -> number  -- Reads a 32-bit float at the given offset
LDataView:getInt16( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads a signed 16-bit integer at the given offset
LDataView:getInt32( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads a signed 32-bit integer at the given offset
LDataView:getInt8( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads a signed 8-bit integer at the given offset
LDataView:getSize() -> integer  -- Returns the size of this view in bytes
LDataView:getUInt16( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads an unsigned 16-bit integer at the given offset
LDataView:getUInt32( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads an unsigned 32-bit integer at the given offset
LDataView:getUInt8( offset : integer ) -> integer  -- Reads an unsigned 8-bit integer at the given offset
LDataView:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LDataView:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

### `LDataWriter`

Write-cursor wrapper for the `lurek.data` module.

```lua
LDataWriter:len() -> integer  -- Returns the total buffer length
LDataWriter:seek( pos : integer ) -> nil  -- Moves the write cursor to the given position
LDataWriter:tell() -> integer  -- Returns the current write cursor position
LDataWriter:toBytes() -> string  -- Returns the buffer contents as a Lua string
LDataWriter:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LDataWriter:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
LDataWriter:writeBytes( value : string ) -> nil  -- Writes raw bytes from a Lua string
LDataWriter:writeF32LE( value : number ) -> nil  -- Writes a 32-bit LE float
LDataWriter:writeF64LE( value : number ) -> nil  -- Writes a 64-bit LE float
LDataWriter:writeI16LE( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes a signed 16-bit LE integer
LDataWriter:writeI32LE( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes a signed 32-bit LE integer
LDataWriter:writeI8( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes a signed 8-bit integer
LDataWriter:writeString( value : string ) -> nil  -- Writes a length-prefixed UTF-8 string (4-byte LE length + bytes)
LDataWriter:writeU16BE( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes an unsigned 16-bit BE integer
LDataWriter:writeU16LE( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes an unsigned 16-bit LE integer
LDataWriter:writeU32LE( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes an unsigned 32-bit LE integer
LDataWriter:writeU8( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Writes an unsigned 8-bit integer
```

### `LRingBuffer`

Lua-side fixed-capacity ring buffer that holds any Lua value.

```lua
LRingBuffer:capacity() -> integer  -- Returns the maximum number of elements the buffer can hold
LRingBuffer:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all elements from the buffer, releasing their registry entries
LRingBuffer:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the buffer contains no elements
LRingBuffer:isFull() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the buffer has reached its capacity
LRingBuffer:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of elements currently in the buffer
LRingBuffer:peek() -> unknown  -- Returns the oldest element without removing it, or nil if empty
LRingBuffer:peekNewest() -> unknown  -- Returns the newest element without removing it, or nil if empty
LRingBuffer:pop() -> unknown  -- Removes and returns the oldest element, or nil if the buffer is empty
LRingBuffer:push( value : any ) -> boolean  -- Pushes a value onto the ring buffer
LRingBuffer:toTable() -> table  -- Returns all elements as an array table ordered oldest-first
LRingBuffer:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LRingBuffer:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

## `lurek.image` {#image}

> `lurek.image` - CPU-side pixel-level image manipulation.
>
> Exposes `ImageData` (RGBA pixel buffers), `CompressedImageData` (DXT/BC/ETC),
> `LayeredImage` (multi-layer compositing), `ProvinceGrid` (colour-keyed region maps),
> and `PaletteLUT` (palette-swap look-up tables). All operations run on the CPU;
> upload to GPU is handled by the render layer.

*Coverage: 102/102 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.image.fromScreen() -> ImageData  -- Returns a screen capture `ImageData` when ready; otherwise queues capture for next frame and returns nil
lurek.image.isCompressed( filename : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the file at the given path is a DDS file
lurek.image.loadImage( path : string ) -> ImageData  -- Loads an ImageData from a LIMG binary file
lurek.image.loadLayered( path : string ) -> LayeredImage  -- Loads a LayeredImage from a LIMG binary file
lurek.image.newCompressedData( filename : string ) -> CompressedImageData  -- Loads compressed texture data from a DDS file
lurek.image.newImageData( ... : integer|string|integer? ) -> ImageData  -- Creates a new blank ImageData or loads one from a file
lurek.image.newImageDataFromBytes( width : integer, height : integer, bytes : string ) -> ImageData  -- Creates an ImageData from a raw RGBA8 byte string. Width Ă— height Ă— 4 bytes required
lurek.image.newLayeredImage( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LayeredImage  -- Creates a new empty LayeredImage canvas with no layers
lurek.image.newPaletteLut() -> PaletteLUT  -- Creates a new empty `PaletteLUT` used to remap colours in an image
lurek.image.newProvinceGrid( filename : string ) -> ProvinceGrid  -- Loads a province map PNG and builds an O(1) spatial index with adjacency data
lurek.image.saveImage( imagedata : ImageData, path : string ) -> nil  -- Saves a flat ImageData to a LIMG binary file at the given path
lurek.image.savePNG( imagedata : ImageData, path : string ) -> nil  -- Saves a flat ImageData as a PNG file at the given path
```

### `LCompressedImageData`

Lua-side wrapper around [`CompressedImageData`].

```lua
LCompressedImageData:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns the width and height of the base mip level
LCompressedImageData:getFormat() -> string  -- Returns the compressed format name string
LCompressedImageData:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the height of the base mip level in pixels
LCompressedImageData:getMipmapCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of mipmap levels stored
LCompressedImageData:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the width of the base mip level in pixels
LCompressedImageData:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LCompressedImageData:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

### `LImageData`

RGBA pixel buffer for software image manipulation, pixel access, and encoding.

```lua
LImageData:alphaMask( factor : number ) -> nil  -- Scales every pixel's alpha channel by factor; use to fade an image in or out uniformly
LImageData:applyPaletteLut( lut : PaletteLUT ) -> nil  -- Applies a `PaletteLUT` to the image in place, replacing exact colour matches
LImageData:blit( src : ImageData, dst_x : integer, dst_y : integer ) -> nil  -- Blits the source ImageData onto this image at (dst_x, dst_y) using Porter-Duff over
LImageData:blur( radius : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a new ImageData with a box blur applied using the given pixel radius
LImageData:brightness( factor : number ) -> nil  -- Adjusts the brightness of every pixel by the given factor (< 1.0 darkens, > 1.0 brightens)
LImageData:contrast( factor : number ) -> nil  -- Adjusts the contrast of every pixel by the given factor (< 1.0 reduces, > 1.0 increases)
LImageData:convolve( kernel : table, ksize : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Applies a custom NxN convolution kernel to the image and returns a new ImageData
LImageData:crop( x : integer, y : integer, w : integer, h : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a new ImageData containing the rectangular sub-region at (x, y) of the given width and height
LImageData:diff( other : ImageData ) -> integer  -- Returns the sum of absolute per-channel pixel differences with another ImageData
LImageData:drawCircle( cx : integer, cy : integer, radius : integer, r : integer, g : integer, b : integer, a : integer ) -> nil  -- Draws a filled circle onto the image
LImageData:drawLine( x0 : integer, y0 : integer, x1 : integer, y1 : integer, r : integer, g : integer, b : integer, a : integer ) -> nil  -- Draws a line using Bresenham's algorithm
LImageData:drawNineSlice( source : ImageData, src_x : integer, src_y : integer, src_w : integer, src_h : integer, dst_x : integer, dst_y : integer, dst_w : integer, dst_h : integer, inset_left : integer, inset_right : integer, inset_top : integer, inset_bottom : integer ) -> nil  -- Draws a nine-slice patch from a source atlas image into this image
LImageData:drawRect( x : integer, y : integer, w : integer, h : integer, r : integer, g : integer, b : integer, a : integer ) -> nil  -- Draws a filled rectangle onto the image
LImageData:encode( format : string ) -> string  -- Encodes the image into a byte string in the specified format (currently "png")
LImageData:fill( r : integer, g : integer, b : integer, a : integer ) -> nil  -- Fills every pixel with the given solid RGBA colour, overwriting all existing content
LImageData:flipHorizontal() -> nil  -- Flips the image left-to-right (mirror across vertical axis), modifying in place
LImageData:flipVertical() -> nil  -- Flips the image top-to-bottom (mirror across horizontal axis), modifying in place
LImageData:gamma( gamma : number ) -> nil  -- Applies gamma correction; values < 1.0 brighten shadows, > 1.0 darken them
LImageData:getDimensions() -> integer  -- Returns the width and height of the image as two integers
LImageData:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the height of the image in pixels
LImageData:getPixel( x : integer, y : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the RGBA colour components of the pixel at (x, y) as four integers (0-255)
LImageData:getRawBytes() -> string  -- Returns the raw RGBA8 pixel data as a Lua string (width Ă— height Ă— 4 bytes)
LImageData:getRegion( x : integer, y : integer, width : integer, height : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a copy of the rectangular sub-region as a new ImageData
LImageData:getString() -> string  -- Returns the raw pixel bytes of the image as a Lua string
LImageData:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the width of the image in pixels
LImageData:grayscale() -> nil  -- Converts the image to grayscale using luminance weights (BT.601)
LImageData:invert() -> nil  -- Inverts every colour channel (subtracts each R/G/B value from 255); alpha is preserved
LImageData:mapPixel( func : function ) -> nil  -- Calls func(x, y, r, g, b, a) for each pixel and writes the returned RGBA back
LImageData:mapPixels( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Applies a function to every pixel in-place
LImageData:noise( amount : integer ) -> nil  -- Adds random noise to every pixel channel; amount controls the maximum per-channel perturbation
LImageData:paste( source : ImageData, dx : integer, dy : integer ) -> nil  -- Copies pixels from `source` onto this image starting at (dx, dy)
LImageData:posterize( levels : integer ) -> nil  -- Reduces each channel to `levels` discrete steps, creating a flat poster-paint look
LImageData:resize( ... : integer|string? ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a bilinear-interpolated copy of the image at the given dimensions
LImageData:resizeNearest( new_w : integer, new_h : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a new ImageData scaled to (new_w, new_h) using nearest-neighbour interpolation
LImageData:rotate90cw() -> ImageData  -- Returns a new ImageData rotated 90 degrees clockwise; the original is not modified
LImageData:saturation( factor : number ) -> nil  -- Adjusts colour saturation; 0.0 produces grayscale, 1.0 is unchanged, > 1.0 boosts saturation
LImageData:sepia() -> nil  -- Applies a warm sepia tone to the image using standard sepia matrix weights
LImageData:setPixel( x : integer, y : integer, r : integer, g : integer, b : integer, a : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the RGBA colour of the pixel at (x, y); returns an error if coordinates are out of bounds
LImageData:setRawData( bytes : string ) -> nil  -- Replaces all pixel data from a raw RGBA byte string
LImageData:sharpen() -> ImageData  -- Returns a new ImageData with a sharpening convolution kernel applied
LImageData:threshold( value : integer ) -> nil  -- Converts the image to black-and-white: pixels above value become white, at or below become black
LImageData:tint( tr : integer, tg : integer, tb : integer, factor : number ) -> nil  -- Blends an RGB tint colour into every pixel, controlled by factor (0.0 = no change, 1.0 = full tint)
LImageData:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LImageData:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type name
```

### `LLayeredImage`

Lua-side wrapper around [`LayeredImage`].

```lua
LLayeredImage:addLayer( name : string? ) -> integer  -- Appends a new blank transparent layer on top and returns its 1-based index
LLayeredImage:getHeight() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas height shared by all layers
LLayeredImage:getLayer( index : integer ) -> ImageData  -- Returns a copy of the layer's pixel buffer as an ImageData
LLayeredImage:getName( index : integer ) -> string  -- Returns the name of a layer
LLayeredImage:getOpacity( index : integer ) -> number  -- Returns the opacity of a layer in [0.0, 1.0]
LLayeredImage:getWidth() -> integer  -- Returns the canvas width shared by all layers
LLayeredImage:isVisible( index : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether a layer is visible
LLayeredImage:layerCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of layers in the stack
LLayeredImage:merge() -> ImageData  -- Flattens all visible layers into a single ImageData using Porter-Duff "over" compositing
LLayeredImage:moveLayer( from_index : integer, to_index : integer ) -> boolean  -- Moves a layer from one position to another, shifting layers in between
LLayeredImage:removeLayer( index : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes the layer at the given 1-based index. Returns true on success
LLayeredImage:save( path : string ) -> nil  -- Saves the layered image to a LIMG binary file at the given path
LLayeredImage:setLayer( index : integer, imagedata : ImageData ) -> boolean  -- Replaces a layer's pixel buffer with a copy of the given ImageData
LLayeredImage:setName( index : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Renames the layer at the given index to the new name string
LLayeredImage:setOpacity( index : integer, opacity : number ) -> boolean  -- Sets the opacity of a layer. Value is clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
LLayeredImage:setVisible( index : integer, visible : boolean ) -> boolean  -- Shows or hides a layer during compositing
LLayeredImage:swapLayers( a : integer, b : integer ) -> boolean  -- Swaps two layers by their 1-based indices, changing their compositing order
LLayeredImage:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LLayeredImage:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

Loads a province map PNG and builds an O(1) spatial index with adjacency data.

```lua
LProvinceGrid:adjacencies() -> table  -- Returns an array of adjacency records. Each record is {province_a, province_b, border_pixels}
LProvinceGrid:borderSegments() -> table  -- Returns merged border segments as

LProvinceGrid:drawShapes( ... : any ) -> nil  -- Draws all province shapes using their original PNG colours
LProvinceGrid:getAt( x : integer, y : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the province ID at pixel coordinates (x, y). Returns 0 for background or out-of-bounds
```

## `lurek.graph` {#graph}

> `lurek.graph` - Directed graph with item-flow simulation.
>
> Provides a node/edge graph model with typed item transport, overflow policies,
> conversion rules, demand/supply links, and event callbacks (`itemEnter`,
> `itemLeave`, etc.). Also exposes graph-level pathfinding (Dijkstra, BFS, A*)
> through the `find*` methods on the `Graph` userdata.

*Coverage: 127/127 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.graph.newGraph() -> Graph  -- Creates a new empty directed graph for item flow simulation
```

### `LGraph`

Lua wrapper around a directed `Graph` with event callback registry.

```lua
LGraph:addEdge( from_ud : Node, to_ud : Node, edge_type : string? ) -> Edge  -- Adds a directed edge between two nodes and returns its handle
LGraph:addItem( item_ud : GraphItem, node_ud : Node ) -> boolean  -- Places an item at a node
LGraph:addNode( node_type : string?, capacity : integer? ) -> Node  -- Adds a node and returns its handle
LGraph:astar( from_node : Node, to_node : Node ) -> table  -- Finds the shortest path between two nodes using A*
LGraph:colorGraph() -> table  -- Assigns each node the smallest non-negative integer colour not shared with any
LGraph:createItem( item_type : string?, decay_time : number? ) -> GraphItem  -- Creates a new unplaced item and returns its handle
LGraph:findPath( from_ud : Node, to_ud : Node ) -> table  -- Finds the shortest path between two nodes using Dijkstra
LGraph:findPathForItem( item_ud : GraphItem, from_ud : Node, to_ud : Node ) -> table  -- Finds the shortest path for a specific item, filtering by item type
LGraph:getComponents() -> table  -- Returns weakly connected components as a table of tables of Node handles
LGraph:getDistance( from_ud : Node, to_ud : Node ) -> number  -- Returns the shortest path distance, or nil if unreachable
LGraph:getEdgeBetween( from_ud : Node, to_ud : Node ) -> Edge  -- Returns the edge between two nodes, or nil if none exists
LGraph:getEdgeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of edges in the graph
LGraph:getEdges() -> table  -- Returns a table of all Edge handles
LGraph:getItemCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the graph
LGraph:getItems() -> table  -- Returns a table of all GraphItem handles
LGraph:getNeighbors( node_ud : Node ) -> table  -- Returns a table of direct neighbor Node handles
LGraph:getNodeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of nodes in the graph
LGraph:getNodes() -> table  -- Returns a table of all Node handles
LGraph:getReachable( from_ud : Node, max_dist : number? ) -> table  -- Returns a table of Node handles reachable from the given node
LGraph:getStats() -> table  -- Returns a statistics snapshot table
LGraph:hasCycle() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the graph contains a directed cycle
LGraph:hasEdge( edge_ud : Edge ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the edge exists in the graph
LGraph:hasItem( item_ud : GraphItem ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the item exists in the graph
LGraph:hasNode( node_ud : Node ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the node exists in the graph
LGraph:isBipartite() -> boolean  -- Returns `true` when the graph can be 2-coloured (bipartite check via BFS)
LGraph:mst() -> table  -- Returns edge IDs forming a minimum spanning tree (Kruskal, undirected view)
LGraph:on( event_name : string, func : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback for a graph simulation event
LGraph:processDemand() -> nil  -- Processes all supply/demand declarations and fires event callbacks
LGraph:removeEdge( edge_ud : Edge ) -> boolean  -- Removes an edge from the graph
LGraph:removeItem( item_ud : GraphItem ) -> boolean  -- Removes an item from the graph entirely
LGraph:removeNode( node_ud : Node ) -> boolean  -- Removes a node from the graph
LGraph:sendItem( item_ud : GraphItem, edge_ud : Edge ) -> boolean  -- Sends an item onto an edge to begin transit
LGraph:step() -> nil  -- Runs one discrete simulation step and fires event callbacks
LGraph:subgraph( nodes : table ) -> Graph  -- Builds a new graph containing only the given node handles and the
LGraph:tickParallel( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances simulation by dt seconds using a parallelised decay phase
LGraph:topologicalSort() -> table  -- Returns a topologically sorted table of Node handles, or nil if a cycle exists
LGraph:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LGraph:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
LGraph:update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances simulation by dt seconds and fires event callbacks
```

### `LGraphEdge`

Lua handle for an edge inside a `Graph`.

```lua
LGraphEdge:addAllowedType( t : string ) -> nil  -- Adds an item type to the edge allow-list
LGraphEdge:clearAllowedTypes() -> nil  -- Clears the edge allow-list so all item types are permitted

LGraphEdge:setCooldown( c : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the cooldown duration in seconds
LGraphEdge:setSpeedModifier( m : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the speed modifier applied to items in transit
LGraphEdge:setThroughput( t : number ) -> nil  -- Sets items per second this edge can transfer
LGraphEdge:setTravelTime( t : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the travel time in seconds for items on this edge
LGraphEdge:setType( t : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the edge type string
LGraphEdge:setWeight( w : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the pathfinding weight of this edge
LGraphEdge:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name "GraphEdge"
LGraphEdge:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the given name matches "GraphEdge" or a parent type
```

### `LGraphItem`

Lua handle for a graph item (typed item) inside a `Graph`.

```lua
LGraphItem:getDecayTime() -> number  -- Returns the decay time in seconds (-1 = immortal)
LGraphItem:getPosition() -> nil  -- Returns the item position: node userdata if at a node, (edge, progress)
```

### `LGraphNode`

Lua handle for a node inside a `Graph`.

```lua
LGraphNode:addDemand( item_type : string, quantity : integer, priority : integer? ) -> nil  -- Declares a demand for the given item type, quantity, and priority
```

## `lurek.ecs` {#ecs}

> `lurek.ecs` - Lightweight ECS with entity lifecycle, components, tags, layers, and blueprints.
>
> Wraps [`crate::ecs::Universe`] as `LuaUniverse` userdata. Supports spawn/kill,
> per-entity component CRUD, tag flags, named layers, queries, blueprints, and
> component add/remove observer callbacks.

*Coverage: 69/69 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.ecs.newUniverse() -> LUniverse  -- Creates a new empty ECS universe
```

### `LUniverse`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Universe`] ECS world.

```lua
LUniverse:addRelation( from : integer, name : string, to : integer ) -> nil  -- Adds a directed named relationship from entity `from` to entity `to`, ignoring duplicates
LUniverse:addSystem( system : table, opts : table? ) -> nil  -- Adds a system table to the universe with an optional priority (lower = earlier), phase,
LUniverse:addTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> nil  -- Attaches a string tag to an entity
LUniverse:applySnapshot( snapshot : table ) -> nil  -- Restores the universe from a snapshot table produced by snapshot.  Alias for deserialize
LUniverse:bitmapTag( id : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Adds a bitmap tag to an entity
LUniverse:bitmapUntag( id : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a bitmap tag from an entity
LUniverse:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all entities, components, tags, layers, and systems. Blueprints are preserved
LUniverse:clearRelations( from : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes all directed named relationships of type `name` from entity `from`
LUniverse:defineBlueprint( name : string, components : table ) -> nil  -- Defines a blueprint from a component table
LUniverse:defineTag( name : string ) -> integer  -- Defines a bitmap tag name, returning its bit index
LUniverse:deserialize( snapshot : table ) -> nil  -- Restores entity state from a snapshot produced by serialize() while preserving blueprints and systems
LUniverse:each( name : string, callback : function ) -> nil  -- Calls callback(id, value) for every entity with the named component
LUniverse:emit( ... : string ) -> nil  -- Emits a named event to all systems that implement the handler, in priority order
LUniverse:extendBlueprint( name : string, parent : string, overrides : table ) -> nil  -- Defines a blueprint by extending a parent with overrides
LUniverse:flushObservers() -> nil  -- Dispatches all pending component-add and component-remove events to registered callbacks
LUniverse:get( id : integer, name : string ) -> table  -- Returns the component value for an entity, or nil if missing
LUniverse:getBitmapTagBit( name : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the bit index for a bitmap tag name, or nil if undefined
LUniverse:getBlueprintComponents( name : string ) -> table  -- Returns a deep copy of a blueprint's component table, or nil
LUniverse:getChildren( parent_id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all direct child entity IDs
LUniverse:getComponents( id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all component names for an entity
LUniverse:getDirtyEntities() -> table  -- Returns entity IDs whose components changed since the last flushObservers / takeObserverEvents call
LUniverse:getEntities() -> table  -- Returns all alive entity IDs
LUniverse:getEntitiesByLayer( layer : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities on a specific layer
LUniverse:getEntitiesByTag( tag : string ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with the given string tag
LUniverse:getEntitiesSorted() -> table  -- Returns all alive entities sorted by layer then ID
LUniverse:getEntityCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of alive entities
LUniverse:getLayer( id : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the layer for an entity, defaulting to zero
LUniverse:getParent( child_id : integer ) -> integer  -- Returns the parent entity ID, or nil if unparented
LUniverse:getRelated( from : integer, name : string ) -> table  -- Returns all entity IDs reachable from `from` via the named relationship
LUniverse:getSystemCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of registered systems
LUniverse:getTags( id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns all string tags for an entity
LUniverse:has( id : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity has the named component
LUniverse:hasBitmapTag( id : integer, name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity has the given bitmap tag
LUniverse:hasBlueprint( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a blueprint with the given name exists
LUniverse:hasRelation( from : integer, name : string, to : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a directed named relationship from `from` to `to` exists
LUniverse:hasTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity carries the given tag
LUniverse:isAlive( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the entity ID is currently alive
LUniverse:kill( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Destroys the entity with the given ID, freeing its slot for reuse
LUniverse:killRecursive( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Kills an entity and all its descendants recursively
LUniverse:listBlueprints() -> table  -- Returns all defined blueprint names
LUniverse:onComponentAdded( name : string, callback : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback for component-added events that is dispatched by flushObservers()
LUniverse:onComponentRemoved( name : string, callback : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback for component-removed events that is dispatched by flushObservers()
LUniverse:query( ... : string ) -> table  -- Returns entity IDs that have all listed component names
LUniverse:queryBitmapAll( names : table ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with all of the listed bitmap tags
LUniverse:queryBitmapAny( names : table ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with any of the listed bitmap tags
LUniverse:queryBitmapTag( name : string ) -> table  -- Returns all alive entities with the given bitmap tag
LUniverse:queryMulti( names : table, callback : function ) -> nil  -- Iterates every alive entity that has ALL the listed components and calls the callback with
LUniverse:queryNot( with_table : table, without_table : table ) -> table  -- Returns entity IDs that have all `with` components and none of the `without` components
LUniverse:release() -> nil  -- Releases all universe state, equivalent to clear
LUniverse:remove( id : integer, name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a component from an entity
LUniverse:removeBlueprint( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a blueprint definition
LUniverse:removeRelation( from : integer, name : string, to : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes the directed named relationship from entity `from` to entity `to`
LUniverse:removeSystem( system : table ) -> nil  -- Removes a system table from the universe
LUniverse:removeTag( id : integer, tag : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a string tag from an entity
LUniverse:render() -> nil  -- Calls render(system, world) on each system in the "render" phase in priority order; falls back to draw(system, world)
LUniverse:serialize() -> table  -- Serializes all alive entities to a Lua table snapshot
LUniverse:set( id : integer, name : string, value : any ) -> nil  -- Sets a component value on an entity
LUniverse:setLayer( id : integer, layer : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the layer for an entity
LUniverse:setParent( child_id : integer, parent_id : integer? ) -> nil  -- Sets or clears the parent of an entity
LUniverse:snapshot() -> table  -- Serializes the entire universe state to a Lua table.  Alias for serialize
LUniverse:spawn() -> integer  -- Creates a new entity and returns its packed ID
LUniverse:spawnBlueprint( name : string, overrides : table? ) -> integer  -- Spawns an entity from a blueprint with optional overrides
LUniverse:spawnBulk( name : string, count : integer, overrides : table? ) -> table  -- Spawns `count` entities from a blueprint, returns an array of entity IDs
LUniverse:takeSnapshotDiff() -> table  -- Returns a lightweight incremental diff of world changes since the last call
LUniverse:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LUniverse:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
LUniverse:update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls update(system, world, dt) on each system in the "update" phase in priority order
LUniverse:updatePhase( phase : string, dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls the named lifecycle method on each system that belongs to the given phase, in priority order
```
