## `lurek.scene` {#scene}

> `lurek.scene` - Scene stack management, transitions, registry, and depth-sorted rendering.

*Coverage: 60/60 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.scene.clear() -> nil  -- Clears all scenes from the stack, calling leave on each
lurek.scene.clearQueuedTransitions() -> nil  -- Clears queued transitions without affecting the currently running transition
lurek.scene.define( def : table? ) -> function  -- Creates a reusable scene class - returns a zero-argument constructor function
lurek.scene.depth() -> integer  -- Returns the number of scenes on the stack; alias for `getStackSize`
lurek.scene.deserializeScene( snapshot : table ) -> nil  -- Restores scene data_refs from a snapshot produced by serializeScene()
lurek.scene.draw() -> nil  -- Draws all scenes in the stack from bottom to top (legacy name; prefer `render`)
lurek.scene.fade( duration : number? ) -> table  -- Pre-built named transition factory functions.  Each function accepts optional
lurek.scene.getActiveScenes() -> table  -- Returns a table array of all active scene tables
lurek.scene.getCurrent() -> table  -- Returns the current top scene table, or nil if the stack is empty
lurek.scene.getCurrentLayer() -> integer  -- Gets processing layer for the current top scene
lurek.scene.getData( key : string ) -> table  -- Returns a value from the inter-scene data store, or nil if not found
lurek.scene.getQueuedTransitionCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of queued transitions waiting in the sequencer
lurek.scene.getRegistered( name : string ) -> table  -- Returns a registered scene table by name, or nil if not found
lurek.scene.getRegisteredNames() -> table  -- Returns a list of all registered scene names
lurek.scene.getStackSize() -> integer  -- Returns the number of scenes on the stack
lurek.scene.getTransitionProgress() -> number  -- Returns the transition progress from 0.0 to 1.0
lurek.scene.getTransitionProgressEased() -> number  -- Returns the easing-adjusted transition progress from 0.0 to 1.0
lurek.scene.getTransitionTypes() -> table  -- Returns a table listing all supported transition type strings
lurek.scene.hasData( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the given key exists in the data store
lurek.scene.hasRegistered( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a scene is registered under the given name
lurek.scene.iris( duration : number? ) -> table  -- Returns an iris in/out (circular reveal) transition config table
lurek.scene.isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the scene stack is empty
lurek.scene.isOverlay() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the current top scene was pushed as an overlay
lurek.scene.isPreloaded( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the named scene has been preloaded
lurek.scene.isTransitioning() -> boolean  -- Returns true if a scene transition is currently active
lurek.scene.new( def : table? ) -> table  -- Creates a scene instance directly from a methods table
lurek.scene.newDepthSorter() -> DepthSorter  -- Creates a new DepthSorter for z-ordered draw batching
lurek.scene.newScene( def : table? ) -> table  -- Alias for `lurek.scene.new`. Creates a scene instance from a methods table
lurek.scene.pop( transition : string?, duration : number?, easing : string? ) -> nil  -- Pops the top scene from the stack with an optional transition and easing
lurek.scene.popTo( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Pops scenes until the named scene is on top, calling leave on each removed
lurek.scene.preload( name : string, loader : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a loader function for a named scene. The loader is called
lurek.scene.process( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls `scene:ready(self)` once per scene on the first tick after enter,
lurek.scene.processLate( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls `scene:process_late(dt)` on all active scenes (after process, before render)
lurek.scene.processPhysics( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Calls `scene:process_physics(dt)` on all active scenes (fixed timestep)
lurek.scene.push( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, easing : string?, params : table? ) -> nil  -- Pushes a scene table onto the stack with an optional transition and easing
lurek.scene.pushOverlay( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, easing : string?, params : table? ) -> nil  -- Pushes a scene as a non-pausing overlay over the current top scene
lurek.scene.pushPreloaded( name : string, transition : string?, duration : number?, easing : string?, params : table? ) -> nil  -- Pushes a registered scene by name, running its loader if not yet preloaded
lurek.scene.queueTransition( transition : string, duration : number, easing : string? ) -> nil  -- Queues a transition to run after the current transition completes
lurek.scene.registerScene( name : string, scene : table ) -> nil  -- Registers a scene table by name for later retrieval
lurek.scene.removeData( key : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a value from the inter-scene data store by key
lurek.scene.render() -> nil  -- Draws all scenes in the stack from bottom to top
lurek.scene.renderUi() -> nil  -- Draws UI overlay for all scenes in the stack from bottom to top
lurek.scene.serializeScene() -> table  -- Returns a snapshot of the scene stack as a Lua table: { stack=[name...], data={key=val} }
lurek.scene.setCurrentLayer( layer : integer ) -> boolean  -- Sets processing layer for the current top scene
lurek.scene.setData( key : string, value : any ) -> nil  -- Stores a value in the inter-scene data store under the given key
lurek.scene.slide( direction : string?, duration : number? ) -> table  -- Returns a directional slide transition config table
lurek.scene.switchTo( scene : table, transition : string?, duration : number?, easing : string?, params : table? ) -> nil  -- Replaces the top scene with a new one, calling leave and enter callbacks
lurek.scene.unregisterScene( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a scene from the registry by name
lurek.scene.update( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Updates the top scene and any active transition (legacy name; prefer `process`)
lurek.scene.wipe( duration : number? ) -> table  -- Returns a wipe/curtain transition config table
```

### `LDepthSorter`

```lua

LDepthSorter:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all registered callbacks without calling them

```

## `lurek.pathfind` {#pathfind}

> `lurek.pathfind` - Grid pathfinding, flow fields, HPA*, and unit-aware navigation.

*Coverage: 101/101 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.pathfind.getThreadCount() -> integer  -- Returns the background pathfinding thread count (currently always 0)
lurek.pathfind.newFlowField( grid : LNavGrid ) -> LFlowField  -- Creates a new FlowField backed by a NavGrid
lurek.pathfind.newHexGrid( width : integer, height : integer, layout : string? ) -> LHexGrid  -- Creates a hex grid for pathfinding, LOS, FOV, and range queries
lurek.pathfind.newJpsGrid( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LJpsGrid  -- Creates a uniform-cost grid optimised for Jump Point Search (orthogonal + diagonal)
lurek.pathfind.newNavGrid( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LNavGrid  -- Creates a new NavGrid with all cells walkable
lurek.pathfind.newNavGridFromTileMap( tilemap : LTileMap, layer_index : integer, blocked_gids : table ) -> LNavGrid  -- Builds a NavGrid from a TileMap layer, treating specified GIDs as blocked (unwalkable)
lurek.pathfind.newNavMesh() -> LNavMesh  -- Creates an empty polygon navmesh for non-tile worlds
lurek.pathfind.newPathFlowField( grid : LPathGrid ) -> LAIFlowField  -- Creates a new BFS flow field from a PathGrid
lurek.pathfind.newPathGrid( w : integer, h : integer, cellSize : number ) -> LPathGrid  -- Creates a new PathGrid with per-cell cost and walkability
lurek.pathfind.newPathfinder( grid : LNavGrid ) -> LUnitPathfinder  -- Creates a new UnitPathfinder backed by a NavGrid
lurek.pathfind.rangeMap( opts : table ) -> table  -- Computes a Dijkstra range-of-movement map from an origin within a movement budget
lurek.pathfind.setThreadCount( count : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the background pathfinding thread count (currently a no-op)
```

## `lurek.minimap` {#minimap}

> `lurek.minimap` - Grid-based minimap with terrain, fog of war, objects, pings, and markers.

*Coverage: 87/87 items documented (100%)*


### `LMinimap`

```lua
LSaveManager:onAfterLoad( func : function? ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback that fires after every successful load operation
LSaveManager:onBeforeSave( func : function? ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback that fires before every save operation

LSaveManager:reset() -> nil  -- Resets all state, removing callbacks and clearing the manager
LSaveManager:restore( data : table ) -> nil  -- Restores data from a table, applying migrations and calling restorers
LSaveManager:save( slot : string ) -> nil  -- Collects data and writes it to a slot file
LSaveManager:setCompress( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables LZ4 compression for saved data
LSaveManager:setSchemaVersion( version : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the current schema version for new saves
LSaveManager:setSummary( summary : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the summary string included in save metadata
LSaveManager:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LSaveManager:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
LSaveManager:unregister( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a named module and its callbacks
```

## `lurek.log` {#log}

> `lurek.log` - Structured game-level logging API.
>
> Exposes convenience logging functions so Lua scripts can emit messages at
> specific severity levels and query or change the active log level at
> runtime. Also supports configurable output sinks (file, memory ring buffer)
> in addition to the default stderr channel, similar to Python's `logging`
> module handlers.

*Coverage: 18/18 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.log.addSink( config : table ) -> integer  -- Creates and registers a new log output sink from the given configuration table
lurek.log.clearSinks() -> nil  -- Removes every registered log sink, returning the logging system to its default state where messages go only to the engine log backend (stderr)
lurek.log.debug( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a message at debug severity to the engine log and all registered sinks
lurek.log.debug_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a structured log message at debug severity with key-value metadata
lurek.log.error( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a message at error severity to the engine log and all registered sinks
lurek.log.error_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a structured log message at error severity with key-value metadata
lurek.log.flushFile( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Forces the operating system to write any buffered data for a file-type sink to disk
lurek.log.getLevel() -> string  -- Returns the name of the current global minimum severity threshold as a lowercase string (e.g
lurek.log.info( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a message at info severity to the engine log and all registered sinks
lurek.log.info_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a structured log message at info severity with key-value metadata
lurek.log.listSinks() -> table  -- Returns an array-like table where each entry is a table describing one registered sink
lurek.log.print( level : string, message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a log message at an arbitrary severity level specified as a string
lurek.log.readMemory( id : integer, drain : boolean? ) -> table  -- Reads log entries stored in a memory-type sink
lurek.log.removeSink( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes a previously registered log sink by its numeric identifier
lurek.log.setLevel( level : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the global minimum severity threshold for the engine log backend
lurek.log.struct( level : string, message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a structured log message that includes arbitrary key-value metadata alongside the human-readable text
lurek.log.warn( message : string, tag : string? ) -> nil  -- Emits a message at warning severity to the engine log and all registered sinks
lurek.log.warn_fields( message : string, fields_table : table ) -> nil  -- Emits a structured log message at warning severity with key-value metadata
```

## `lurek.debugbridge` {#debugbridge}

> `lurek.debugbridge` - TCP debug server bindings for runtime inspection and control.
>
> Embeds a JSON-over-TCP server (127.0.0.1 only) inside the running game.
> External tools (VS Code extension, MCP server) connect to inspect and
> control the game at runtime. Network I/O runs on a background thread;
> Lua-dependent methods are queued and dispatched via `poll()` on the main thread.

*Coverage: 16/16 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.debugbridge.broadcast( event : string, json_data : string ) -> nil  -- Broadcasts a JSON event to all connected clients
lurek.debugbridge.capturePrint( msg : string, source : string?, line : integer? ) -> nil  -- Captures a print message and broadcasts it to connected clients
lurek.debugbridge.clearPrintHistory() -> nil  -- Clears the print history
lurek.debugbridge.consumeHotReloadRequest() -> boolean  -- Consumes and clears a pending remote hot-reload request
lurek.debugbridge.getClientCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of connected TCP clients
lurek.debugbridge.getPerformance() -> table  -- Returns performance statistics
lurek.debugbridge.getPort() -> integer  -- Returns the server port (0 if not running)
lurek.debugbridge.getPrintHistory( count : integer? ) -> table  -- Returns the print history
lurek.debugbridge.getProtocolInfo() -> table  -- Returns bridge protocol information including version and capabilities
lurek.debugbridge.isRunning() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the server is currently running
lurek.debugbridge.isScreenshotRequested() -> boolean  -- Returns whether a screenshot is currently requested
lurek.debugbridge.poll() -> nil  -- Poll for pending Lua-dependent requests from TCP clients
lurek.debugbridge.requestScreenshot( scale : integer? ) -> nil  -- Flags a screenshot request for the next frame
lurek.debugbridge.setMaxPrintHistory( max : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the maximum print history size
lurek.debugbridge.start( port : integer? ) -> boolean  -- Start the TCP debug server on 127.0.0.1:port
lurek.debugbridge.stop() -> nil  -- Stop the TCP debug server and close all connections
```

```lua
lurek.docs.coverageModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : LApiCatalog? ) -> integer  -- Return (documented_count, total_live_count) for a single module
lurek.docs.describe( qualified_name : string, description : string ) -> nil  -- Inject or update a description for a named API entry
lurek.docs.exportAll( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog, output_dir : string ) -> nil  -- Export completions.json, hover.json, and signatures.json to a directory
lurek.docs.exportCheatsheet( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog, path : string ) -> nil  -- Export a one-line-per-function plain-text cheatsheet
lurek.docs.exportCompletions( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog, path : string ) -> nil  -- Export VS Code IntelliSense completions JSON to a file
lurek.docs.exportHover( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog, path : string ) -> nil  -- Export VS Code hover JSON to a file
lurek.docs.exportMarkdown( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog, path : string ) -> nil  -- Export a Markdown API reference file
lurek.docs.exportSignatures( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog, path : string ) -> nil  -- Export VS Code signature-help JSON to a file
lurek.docs.getCatalog() -> LApiCatalog  -- Return the current internal catalog as an ApiCatalog userdata
lurek.docs.loadAll( directory : string ) -> LApiCatalog  -- Load all .toml files in a directory and merge into a single ApiCatalog
lurek.docs.loadToml( path : string ) -> LApiCatalog  -- Load a TOML doc file into an ApiCatalog
lurek.docs.quality( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog? ) -> LQualityReport  -- Calculate quality metrics for a catalog or the internal catalog
lurek.docs.qualityModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : LApiCatalog? ) -> LQualityReport  -- Calculate quality metrics for a single module
lurek.docs.reflectLive( ns : string? ) -> table  -- Walks the live lurek.* Lua table and returns a structured reflection table
lurek.docs.reflectTable( tbl : table, name : string? ) -> table  -- Reflects any Lua table and returns a structure describing its keys and value types
lurek.docs.resetCatalog() -> nil  -- Clear all entries from the internal catalog
lurek.docs.scan( opts : table? ) -> LApiCatalog  -- Scan the lurek.* namespace to build an API catalog from live bindings
lurek.docs.scanModule( module_name : string ) -> LApiCatalog  -- Scan a single module's bindings
lurek.docs.schema( rules : table, name : string? ) -> LSchema  -- Creates a schema validator from a rules table
lurek.docs.schemaFromToml( toml_text : string ) -> LSchema  -- Creates a schema validator from TOML text
lurek.docs.setParamInfo( qualified_name : string, params : table ) -> nil  -- Set the parameter metadata for a catalog entry
lurek.docs.setReturnInfo( qualified_name : string, returns : table ) -> nil  -- Set the return type metadata for a catalog entry
lurek.docs.validate( catalog_ud : LApiCatalog? ) -> LValidationReport  -- Validate catalog completeness against the live lurek.* bindings
lurek.docs.validateModule( module_name : string, catalog_ud : LApiCatalog? ) -> LValidationReport  -- Validate a single module against the live lurek.<module>.* bindings
```

### `LApiCatalog`

Wraps a catalog snapshot of API entries for Lua access.

```lua
LApiCatalog:entryCount( module : string? ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries, optionally scoped to a module
LApiCatalog:filter( predicate : function ) -> LApiCatalog  -- Returns a new catalog containing only entries for which predicate returns true
LApiCatalog:getEntries( module : string? ) -> table  -- Returns all entries, optionally filtered to a single module
LApiCatalog:getEntry( qualified_name : string ) -> LDocEntry  -- Returns a single entry by qualified name, or nil
LApiCatalog:getModules() -> table  -- Returns a sorted list of module names present in the catalog
LApiCatalog:getTypeMethods( qualified_name : string ) -> table  -- Returns entries that are methods of the given type qualified name
LApiCatalog:getTypes( module_name : string ) -> table  -- Returns the names of all entries with kind "type" in the given module
LApiCatalog:merge( other : userdata ) -> LApiCatalog  -- Returns a new catalog that is the union of this and another catalog, with other overriding duplicates
LApiCatalog:search( query : string ) -> table  -- Returns a table of entries whose name, qualified name, or description contains query
LApiCatalog:toJSON() -> string  -- Serialises the catalog to a pretty-printed JSON string
LApiCatalog:toTable() -> table  -- Converts the catalog to a plain Lua table array
LApiCatalog:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LApiCatalog:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

```lua
LDocEntry:getDeprecated() -> string  -- Returns the deprecation message, or nil

LDocEntry:getKind() -> string  -- Returns the kind tag for this entry (e.g. `'function'`, `'method'`, `'class'`)

LDocEntry:getReturns() -> table  -- Returns the return values as a table of `{type, description}` records
LDocEntry:getScore() -> number  -- Returns the quality score in [0,1]
LDocEntry:getSince() -> string  -- Returns the since version string, or nil

LDocEntry:hasExample() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry has an example snippet
LDocEntry:hasParameters() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry has at least one parameter
LDocEntry:hasReturnType() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the entry declares at least one return type
LDocEntry:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LDocEntry:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

```lua
LQualityReport:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

```lua
LSchema:assert( data : table ) -> nil  -- Validates data and throws a Lua error on failure with all error messages joined
```

### `LValidationReport`

Wraps a validation report for Lua access.

```lua
LValidationReport:getIncomplete() -> table  -- Returns the list of qualified names whose catalog entry is incomplete
```

---

## `lurek.patterns` {#patterns}

> `lurek.patterns` - Software design patterns API.
>
> Provides factory functions and UserData wrappers for reusable patterns:
> EventBus (prioritized pub-sub), ObjectPool (object reuse), CommandStack
> (undo/redo), ServiceLocator (DI container), Factory (named constructors),
> and SimpleState (finite state machine).

*Coverage: 242/242 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.patterns.newBehaviorTree() -> LBehaviorTree  -- Creates a new behaviour tree for composing AI logic with Sequence, Selector, Parallel, Inverter, Repeat, and Leaf nodes
lurek.patterns.newBlackboard( name : string? ) -> LBlackboard  -- Creates a new Blackboard shared key-value store
lurek.patterns.newCommandStack( max_size : integer? ) -> LCommandStack  -- Creates a new CommandStack instance
lurek.patterns.newDebounce( wait : number ) -> LDebounce  -- Creates a trailing-edge debounce that fires after the input stream is idle for wait seconds
lurek.patterns.newEventBus( name : string? ) -> LEventBus  -- Creates a new EventBus instance
lurek.patterns.newFactory() -> LFactory  -- Creates a new Factory instance
lurek.patterns.newFunnel( window : number, max_entries : integer?, name : string? ) -> LFunnel  -- Creates a time-windowed event aggregator that flushes on every push when `window` is `0`
lurek.patterns.newGraph( undirected : boolean? ) -> LGraph  -- Creates a directed graph (or undirected when `undirected` is true)
lurek.patterns.newList() -> LList  -- Creates an ordered, resizable list
lurek.patterns.newMap() -> LMap  -- Creates a string-keyed map/dictionary
lurek.patterns.newMediator() -> LMediator  -- Creates a new named-channel message broker
lurek.patterns.newObjectPool() -> LObjectPool  -- Creates a new ObjectPool instance
lurek.patterns.newObserver( name : string? ) -> LObserver  -- Creates a new reactive property Observer
lurek.patterns.newPriorityQueue( name : string? ) -> LPriorityQueue  -- Creates a stable priority-ordered task queue
lurek.patterns.newQueue( capacity : integer? ) -> LQueue  -- Creates a FIFO queue. capacity=0 means unlimited
lurek.patterns.newRelationshipManager() -> LRelationshipManager  -- Creates a new entity relationship manager
lurek.patterns.newRing( capacity : integer, name : string? ) -> LRing  -- Creates a fixed-capacity circular history buffer
lurek.patterns.newServiceLocator() -> LServiceLocator  -- Creates a new ServiceLocator instance
lurek.patterns.newSet() -> LSet  -- Creates an unordered set that rejects duplicate values (by string key)
lurek.patterns.newSimpleState() -> LSimpleState  -- Creates a new SimpleState finite state machine instance
lurek.patterns.newStack( capacity : integer? ) -> LStack  -- Creates a LIFO stack. capacity=0 means unlimited
lurek.patterns.newStrategy() -> LStrategy  -- Creates a new strategy registry
lurek.patterns.newThrottle( interval : number ) -> LThrottle  -- Creates a leading-edge rate limiter that fires at most once per interval seconds
lurek.patterns.newWeightedRandom() -> LWeightedRandom  -- Creates a weighted random selector for loot tables and probability-weighted picks
```

### `LBehaviorTree`

Lua wrapper for the BehaviorTree pattern.

```lua
LBehaviorTree:addChild( parent_id : integer, child_id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Attaches a child node to a parent node
LBehaviorTree:addInverter( label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds an Inverter decorator node
LBehaviorTree:addLeaf( name : string, label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds a Leaf node backed by a named callback
LBehaviorTree:addParallel( min_success : integer, label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds a Parallel composite node
LBehaviorTree:addRepeat( count : integer, label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds a Repeat decorator node
LBehaviorTree:addSelector( label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds a Selector composite node
LBehaviorTree:addSequence( label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds a Sequence composite node
LBehaviorTree:clearAll() -> nil  -- Resets the tree and all registered leaf callbacks
LBehaviorTree:nodeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of nodes in the tree
LBehaviorTree:resetState() -> nil  -- Resets running state and repeat counters
LBehaviorTree:setLeaf( name : string, callback : function ) -> nil  -- Registers the callback for a named leaf node
LBehaviorTree:setRoot( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Sets the root node of the tree
LBehaviorTree:tick() -> string  -- Ticks the behaviour tree once from the root node
```

### `LBlackboard`

Lua wrapper for the Blackboard pattern.

```lua
LBlackboard:clear( key : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a fact from the blackboard
LBlackboard:clearAll() -> nil  -- Clears all facts from the blackboard
LBlackboard:get( key : string )  -- Gets a fact from the blackboard
LBlackboard:getRevision() -> integer  -- Returns the monotonic revision counter (incremented on every write)
LBlackboard:has( key : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when the key has a non-nil value
LBlackboard:keys() -> table  -- Returns all set fact keys as a table
LBlackboard:set( key : string, value : any ) -> nil  -- Sets a fact on the blackboard
LBlackboard:snapshot() -> table  -- Returns all facts as a flat key-value table
LBlackboard:unwatch( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a watcher subscription by id
LBlackboard:watch( key : string, callback : function ) -> integer  -- Subscribes to changes on a specific key (or "*" for all changes)
```

### `LCommandStack`

Lua wrapper for the CommandStack pattern.

```lua
LCommandStack:canRedo() -> boolean  -- Returns true if there is a command available to redo
LCommandStack:canUndo() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the most recent command can be undone
LCommandStack:clearAll() -> nil  -- Clears all command history, releasing Lua registry values
LCommandStack:execute( name : string, exec_fn : function, undo_fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Executes a named command and records it in undo/redo history
LCommandStack:getCurrentName() -> string  -- Returns the name of the most recently executed command
LCommandStack:getHistorySize() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of recorded commands (undo + redo)
LCommandStack:redo() -> boolean  -- Re-executes the next undone command and returns whether it succeeded
LCommandStack:undo() -> boolean  -- Undoes the most recent command and returns whether it succeeded
```

### `LDebounce`

Lua wrapper for the Debounce pattern.

```lua
LDebounce:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels the pending trigger without firing
LDebounce:getFireCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of times this debounce has fired
LDebounce:isPending() -> boolean  -- Returns true when a trigger is pending
LDebounce:onFire( callback : function ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback invoked when the debounce fires
LDebounce:trigger() -> nil  -- Records an input event, resetting the idle timer
LDebounce:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the idle timer by dt seconds; fires the callback if idle wait expired
```

### `LEventBus`

Lua wrapper for the EventBus pattern.

```lua
LEventBus:clear( event : string ) -> nil  -- Removes all listeners for a specific event
LEventBus:clearAll() -> nil  -- Removes all listeners on this EventBus
LEventBus:emit( event : string, ... : any ) -> nil  -- Dispatches an event, calling all registered listeners in priority order
LEventBus:getEvents() -> table  -- Returns all event names that have at least one listener
LEventBus:getListenerCount( event : string ) -> integer  -- Returns the number of listeners registered for an event
LEventBus:off( id : integer ) -> nil  -- Removes a previously registered event listener by subscription ID
LEventBus:on( event : string, callback : function, priority : integer? ) -> integer  -- Registers a listener callback for an event
```

### `LFactory`

Lua wrapper for the Factory pattern.

```lua
LFactory:alias( alias : string, canonical : string ) -> nil  -- Registers an alias pointing to an existing canonical type name
LFactory:clearAll() -> nil  -- Removes all registered type constructors and aliases
LFactory:create( type_name : string, ... : any )  -- Creates an instance of the named type by invoking its constructor
LFactory:getTypes() -> table  -- Returns a table of all registered type names
LFactory:has( type_name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if the named type (or alias) is registered
LFactory:register( type_name : string, ctor : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a named type constructor function
LFactory:remove( type_name : string ) -> nil  -- Unregisters a type constructor (and any aliases pointing to it)
```

### `LFunnel`

Lua wrapper for the Funnel (event aggregator) pattern.

```lua
LFunnel:discard() -> nil  -- Discards all buffered entries without flushing
LFunnel:flush() -> nil  -- Manually flushes all pending entries, invoking the onFlush callback
LFunnel:getFlushCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of flushes performed
LFunnel:onFlush( callback : function ) -> nil  -- Sets a callback invoked when the funnel flushes. Receives a table of {tag, value} entries
LFunnel:pendingCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of buffered entries not yet flushed
LFunnel:push( tag : string, value : number? ) -> nil  -- Adds an event to the funnel, flushing immediately when `max_entries` is reached or `window` is `0`
LFunnel:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the window timer by dt seconds; flushes when window expires
```

### `LGraph`

Lua wrapper for the Graph pattern.

```lua
LGraph:addEdge( from : integer, to : integer, weight : number?, label : string? ) -> integer  -- Adds an edge between two nodes
LGraph:addNode( label : string?, value : any ) -> integer  -- Adds a node with an optional label and payload
LGraph:bfs( start : integer ) -> table  -- Runs a breadth-first search from a node and returns visited IDs in BFS order
LGraph:clearAll() -> nil  -- Removes all nodes, edges, and stored payloads
LGraph:dfs( start : integer ) -> table  -- Runs a depth-first search from a node and returns visited IDs in DFS order
LGraph:edgeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of stored edge records
LGraph:getNodeValue( id : integer )  -- Returns the payload stored for a node
LGraph:hasNode( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true when a node with the given ID exists
LGraph:isConnected( from : integer, to : integer ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if a path exists from `from` to `to`
LGraph:neighbors( id : integer ) -> table  -- Returns the IDs of all direct outgoing neighbours of a node
LGraph:nodeCount() -> integer  -- Returns the number of nodes in the graph
LGraph:removeEdge( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes all edges with the given ID
LGraph:removeNode( id : integer ) -> boolean  -- Removes a node and all its incident edges
```

```lua
LQueue:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the queue is empty
LQueue:isFull() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the queue is at its capacity limit
LQueue:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items in the queue
LQueue:peekAt( index : integer )  -- Returns the value at a 1-based index (front=1)
```


### `LRing`

Lua wrapper for the Ring (circular buffer) pattern.

```lua

LRing:clear() -> nil  -- Removes all entries from the ring
LRing:isFull() -> boolean  -- Returns true when the ring is at capacity

LRing:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of entries currently in the ring

LRing:sum() -> number  -- Returns the sum of all numeric values in the ring
```


### `LStack`

Lua wrapper for a LIFO stack.

```lua

LStack:isEmpty() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the stack is empty

LStack:len() -> integer  -- Returns the number of items on the stack

LStack:peek()  -- Returns the top value without removing it

LStack:peekBottom()  -- Returns the bottom value without removing it
LStack:pop()  -- Removes and returns the top value

LStack:pushBottom( value : any ) -> boolean  -- Pushes a value onto the bottom of the stack
LStack:removeAt( index : integer )  -- Removes and returns a value at a 1-based index
LStack:toArray() -> table  -- Returns all items as a Lua table (bottom to top)
```

## `lurek.camera` {#camera}

> `lurek.camera` - Camera2D creation and manipulation for 2D viewport control.
>
> Each `Camera2D` has position, zoom, rotation, viewport bounds, optional world-space
> clamping, waypoint path following, smooth zoom tweening, per-layer parallax factors,
> and screen-world coordinate transforms.

*Coverage: 88/88 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.camera.new( viewport_w : number?, viewport_h : number? ) -> LCamera  -- Creates a new Camera2D with the given viewport dimensions
lurek.camera.newCamera( viewport_w : number?, viewport_h : number? ) -> LCamera  -- Creates a new 2D camera with the given viewport dimensions
lurek.camera.newRig() -> LCameraRig  -- Creates a multi-camera rig for split-screen, minimap, and PiP orchestration
```

### `LCamera`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Camera2D`] instance.

```lua

LCamera:getFollowSmooth() -> number  -- Returns current follow smoothing factor
LCamera:getLookAhead() -> number  -- Returns current look-ahead multiplier
LCamera:getParallaxFactor( layer : string ) -> number  -- Returns the parallax scroll factor for the named render layer
LCamera:getPosition() -> number  -- Returns the camera's current world-space position as two values
LCamera:getRenderOffset() -> number  -- Returns canonical render offset (sway + shake) as x,y
LCamera:getRotation() -> number  -- Returns the camera's current rotation angle in radians

LCamera:getTarget() -> boolean  -- Returns current target as `(ok, x, y)`
LCamera:getViewport() -> number  -- Returns the current screen-space viewport rectangle as four values

LCamera:isSway() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the sway oscillation effect is currently running

LCamera:presetBalancedFollow() -> nil  -- Configures a balanced follow setup: moderate response, medium dead zone
LCamera:presetCinematicFollow() -> nil  -- Configures a cinematic follow setup: slow response, large dead zone, no look-ahead

LCamera:setDeadZone( w : number, h : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the dead zone half-extents for camera follow
LCamera:setFollowEasing( easing : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the easing mode used by smooth follow interpolation
LCamera:setFollowSmooth( speed : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the follow interpolation speed for smooth camera tracking
LCamera:setLookAhead( mul : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the look-ahead multiplier for predictive camera follow
LCamera:setParallaxFactor( layer : string, factor : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the parallax scroll factor for the named render layer
LCamera:setPosition( x : number, y : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the camera's world-space position to the given coordinates

LCamera:setRotationConstraints( min_rot : number?, max_rot : number? ) -> nil  -- Sets minimum and maximum rotation constraints in radians

LCamera:startSway( amplitude_x : number, amplitude_y : number, frequency : number, decay : number? ) -> nil  -- Starts a sinusoidal x/y offset oscillation for ambient camera motion (e.g
LCamera:stopBreathing() -> nil  -- Stops the active breathing zoom oscillation effect immediately
LCamera:stopPath() -> nil  -- Cancels the active camera path animation immediately, leaving the camera at its current position along the path
LCamera:stopSway() -> nil  -- Stops the active sway oscillation effect immediately, resetting the camera's offset back to zero
LCamera:stopZoom() -> nil  -- Cancels the active smooth zoom tween immediately, leaving the camera at its current zoom level
LCamera:toScreen( wx : number, wy : number ) -> number  -- Converts world-space coordinates to screen-space pixel coordinates accounting for the camera's position, zoom, rotation, and viewport
```

## `lurek.devtools` {#devtools}

> `lurek.devtools` - Runtime diagnostics and developer tools.
>
> Thin Lua bridge that delegates to the [`devtools`][crate::devtools] domain module.
> All state management happens in [`crate::devtools`]; this file only converts
> between Lua values and domain types.

*Coverage: 62/62 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.devtools.clearLog() -> nil  -- Discards all accumulated log entries from the in-memory devtools log buffer
lurek.devtools.clearWatches() -> nil  -- Clears all watched paths
lurek.devtools.debug( message : string ) -> nil  -- Logs a message at DEBUG level
lurek.devtools.error( message : string ) -> nil  -- Logs a message at ERROR level
lurek.devtools.eval( code : string ) -> boolean  -- Evaluates a Lua string and returns (success, results...)
```

## `lurek.effect` {#effect}

> `lurek.effect` - Composable visual effects: post-processing pipeline and screen overlays.
>
> Exposes `PostFxEffect` (individual shader passes), `PostFxStack` (ordered chains),
> `ImageEffect` (named preset bundles), `Overlay` (screen-space weather/vignette),
> and `ScreenTransition` (fade/wipe between scenes). Also provides render-to-texture
> feedback loops for motion-trail effects.

*Coverage: 161/161 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.effect.getEffectTypes() -> table  -- Returns the list of all built-in effect type names
lurek.effect.getShaderErrorDisplay() -> boolean  -- Returns whether shader error display is currently enabled
lurek.effect.newCustomEffect( shader_id : integer ) -> LPostFxEffect  -- Creates a custom shader post-processing effect
lurek.effect.newEffect( type_name : string ) -> LPostFxEffect  -- Creates a new built-in post-processing effect by type name
lurek.effect.newImageEffect( ... : any ) -> LImageEffect  -- Creates a new per-image effect chain
lurek.effect.newOverlay( width : integer, height : integer ) -> LOverlay  -- Creates a new screen overlay controller for weather, flash, shake, and fade effects
lurek.effect.newPass( shader_id : integer ) -> LPostFxEffect  -- Creates a custom-shader post-processing effect (alias for newCustomEffect)
lurek.effect.newPresetStack( name : string, width : integer?, height : integer? ) -> LPostFxStack  -- Creates a pre-configured effect stack from a named preset
lurek.effect.newStack( width : integer?, height : integer? ) -> LPostFxStack  -- Creates a new post-processing pipeline stack
lurek.effect.newTransition( kind : string?, duration : number?, color : table? ) -> LScreenTransition  -- Creates a new screen-transition controller
lurek.effect.setShaderErrorDisplay( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables on-screen shader error display
```

## `lurek.light` {#light}

> `lurek.light` - 2D lighting, shadow occluders, and ambient control.
>
> Manages `Light` handles (point / directional / spot) and `Occluder` handles
> (line-segment shadow casters) within the engine's `LightWorld`. Supports
> attenuation, blend modes, falloff curves, shadow filtering, flicker configs,
> cookie textures, colour transitions, and ambient colour control.

*Coverage: 98/98 items documented (100%)*

```lua
lurek.light.advanceFlickers( dt : number ) -> nil  -- Advances flicker phase for all lights with flicker enabled
lurek.light.clear() -> nil  -- Removes all lights and occluders, resets ambient to default

lurek.light.isEnabled() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the lighting system is active

lurek.light.setAmbient( r : number, g : number, b : number, a : number? ) -> nil  -- Sets the global ambient light color
lurek.light.setEnabled( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets whether the lighting system is active
lurek.light.setGroupColor( groupId : integer, r : number, g : number, b : number, a : number? ) -> nil  -- Sets the color for all lights in the given group
lurek.light.setGroupEnabled( groupId : integer, enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets the enabled state for all lights in the given group
lurek.light.setGroupIntensity( groupId : integer, intensity : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the intensity for all lights in the given group
```

### `LPipeline`

Lua-side wrapper around a [`Pipeline`] DAG.

```lua
LPipeline:addBranch( name : string, deps : table, when_fn : function, then_fn : function, else_fn : function? ) -> LPipeline  -- Adds an if/else branch as two conditional steps that share one predicate result
LPipeline:addConditional( name : string, deps : table, fn : function, when_fn : function ) -> LPipeline  -- Adds a conditional step to the pipeline
LPipeline:addStep( step : LPipelineStep ) -> LPipeline  -- Adds a step to the pipeline
LPipeline:addSubPipeline( sub_pipeline : LPipeline, alias : string, outer_deps : table? ) -> nil  -- Inlines all steps from a sub-pipeline into this pipeline
LPipeline:cancel() -> nil  -- Cancels all pending and waiting steps
LPipeline:clear() -> nil  -- Clears all steps from the pipeline
LPipeline:getContext() -> table  -- Returns the stored asynchronous context table
LPipeline:getErrorMode() -> string  -- Returns the current error mode
LPipeline:getExecutionOrder() -> table  -- Returns the topological execution order
LPipeline:getName() -> string  -- Returns the pipeline name
LPipeline:getParallelGroups() -> table  -- Returns the pipeline's parallel execution groups
LPipeline:getResult() -> table  -- Returns the current result table
LPipeline:getStep( name : string ) -> LPipelineStep  -- Returns the step wrapper for the named step
LPipeline:getStepCount() -> integer  -- Returns the total number of steps
LPipeline:getSteps() -> table  -- Returns all step wrappers in the pipeline
LPipeline:getStepsByTag( tag : string ) -> table  -- Returns all steps with a matching tag
LPipeline:isComplete() -> boolean  -- Returns whether all steps have reached a terminal state
LPipeline:isRunning() -> boolean  -- Returns whether the pipeline is running asynchronously
LPipeline:onEvent( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback invoked for lifecycle events (`step_started`, `step_finished`)
LPipeline:onProgress( fn : function ) -> nil  -- Registers a callback invoked after every step
LPipeline:removeStep( name : string ) -> nil  -- Removes a step from the pipeline by name
LPipeline:reset() -> nil  -- Resets all step states and clears async pipeline state
LPipeline:run( context : table? ) -> table  -- Executes the pipeline synchronously in topological order
LPipeline:runAsync( context : table? ) -> nil  -- Starts an asynchronous pipeline run
LPipeline:setErrorMode( mode : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the pipeline error mode
LPipeline:setName( name : string ) -> nil  -- Renames the pipeline without changing its steps, dependencies, or current runtime state
LPipeline:setOnComplete( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback invoked when the pipeline completes
LPipeline:setOnStepComplete( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback invoked when a step completes successfully
LPipeline:setOnStepError( fn : function? ) -> nil  -- Sets the callback invoked when a step fails
LPipeline:toAscii() -> string  -- Returns an ASCII diagram of the pipeline DAG
LPipeline:toTable() -> table  -- Serialises the pipeline definition to a Lua table
LPipeline:type() -> string  -- Returns the Lua-visible type name for this pipeline
LPipeline:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns whether the given type name matches this pipeline
LPipeline:update( dt : number ) -> boolean  -- Advances the asynchronous pipeline by one tick
LPipeline:validate() -> boolean  -- Validates the pipeline dependency graph
```

```lua
LPipelineStep:setOptional( optional : boolean ) -> nil  -- Sets whether this step is optional

LPipelineStep:setRetryDelay( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Sets the delay between retry attempts
LPipelineStep:setTag( tag : string ) -> nil  -- Sets the tag on this step
LPipelineStep:setTimeout( seconds : number ) -> nil  -- Stores a timeout value in this step's metadata
```


### `LTween`

A managed interpolation from start to end values over time.

```lua
LTween:await( ud : any ) -> nil  -- Yields the current coroutine until this tween completes or is cancelled
LTween:cancel( ud : any ) -> nil  -- Cancels this tween immediately; fires the `onCancel` callback if set
LTween:getDuration() -> number  -- Returns configured tween duration in seconds
LTween:getElapsed() -> number  -- Returns elapsed tween time in seconds
LTween:getFields() -> table  -- Returns animated field names
LTween:getProgress() -> number  -- Returns raw 0..1 playback progress (not eased, not accounting for yoyo)
LTween:getRemaining() -> number  -- Returns remaining tween time in seconds
LTween:isActive() -> boolean  -- Returns true if the tween is still running (not completed or cancelled)
LTween:onCancel( self : LTween, f : function ) -> LTween  -- Sets a callback called when the tween is cancelled
LTween:onComplete( self : LTween, f : function ) -> LTween  -- Sets a callback to fire when the tween finishes all cycles
LTween:onUpdate( self : LTween, f : function ) -> LTween  -- Sets a callback called every tick with the current eased progress
LTween:pause() -> nil  -- Pauses this tween; time stops advancing but the tween is not cancelled
LTween:relative( self : LTween, enabled : boolean ) -> LTween  -- Chainable alias for `setRelative`
LTween:resume() -> nil  -- Resumes a paused tween, continuing from the position where it was paused
LTween:setRelative( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables delta mode where field values are interpreted as offsets from start values
LTween:setRepeat( n : integer ) -> nil  -- Sets the number of extra play cycles after the first (0 = play once, -1 = infinite)
LTween:setYoyo( enabled : boolean ) -> nil  -- Enables or disables yoyo (ping-pong) on each repeat cycle
LTween:type() -> string  -- Returns the type name of this object
LTween:typeOf( name : string ) -> boolean  -- Returns true if this object is of the given type
```

A group of animations that run simultaneously over the same duration.

```lua

LTweenParallel:tween( self : LTweenParallel, duration : number, target : table, fields : table, easing : string? ) -> LTweenParallel  -- Creates and adds an inline tween entry to the parallel group

```

### `LTweenSequence`

A chained sequence of animations that run one after another.

```lua

```
